at Ruplastica 2023 Jan 24-27 and then install it at Oldeng factory in Russia.

Description of Machines
1. THERMOFORMING Machines – PF1 Series
a. Summary
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements suited for CE certification
• The machine has automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency is achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be halogen
flash type controlled using special heater controller
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 23

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
b. Machine Description
I. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system directly via the HMI
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 23

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ There are CNC machined pusher boxes with Linear Rail Guides that move front & back by the
connected servo motor that move the universal frames
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on 2 x sides – left & right & 32 mm
thick Steel Flat part in front & back
§ The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature control
§ The top frame also has copper pipes embedded in front & back and also on the cross member
§ frame. The cross-member will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet.
§ MTC with chiller has to be connected to the copper pipes – to be purchased separately
§ There will also be small air jet nozzles in the upper frame for cooling the frame after the heating
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The base plate made of aluminum can be taken out from the machine since it will be resting on ball
transfer units
§ The base plate will be then clamped pneumatically onto the machine
§ This allows sliding of the tool into the machine and makes it a 1 x man operation – quick tool change
system with pneumatic tool clamping
§ There should be a tool change trolley with ball transfer units – part of the quote of the tool
§ The machine will have pneumatic system to lift the base plate and allow to slide into the machine which
then drops down for locking with the machine
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 23

[PAGE 8]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
II. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ There will be sheet centering system in the automatic sheet loader to align the sheets – using
leadscrew mechanism with 2 x servo motors that move in XY direction
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 8 of 23

[PAGE 9]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heaters are servo motor driven but there will be pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of
power failure for 2-level safety
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Halogen Flash Type – Quick
Response – Energy Saving – due to fast
response, they can be switched off when at the
back during the forming cycle
Heater Control via Heatronik special heater control where you can
see if any heater element is not working directly
on the HMI
Heater Movement Type Servo Motor Driven but also will have pneumatic
cylinders to retract the heaters in-case of power
failure (for safety)
Sheet Temperature Sensing Using Pyrometer – isolated from the heater oven
using special construction
c. Sheet Clamping
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 9 of 23

[PAGE 10]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The sheet is clamped tightly on all 4 x sides using strong rack & pinion mechanism
§ There are 4 x racks moving in sync. via 4 x servo motors & gearboxes and vertical travel using
guides
§ There is a pneumatic lock to lock the clamp frame when on the upper position – for safety
§ The motors used are with electrical brakes
d. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the sheet from touhing the bottom heater and also to prevent the sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell – in the upper area - manually height
adjustable for bubble height detection & Timer Control
for blowing air to make the bubble – available on the
touchscreen HMI
ii Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center– approx.. 150 mm below the
sheet level & 50 mm above the bottom heater

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine

Machine 1: Press Machine
There will be a press to give shape to the bio-cellulose material using match mould.
§ Basic Mild Steel welded machine frame – compact design
§ Electrical system – PLC control
§ Control panel 10" touchscreen (HMI) with SD Card to save Recipes
§ Electrical Cabinet - as per CE guidelines
§ The press is composed of 2 x tables – lower & upper
§ Lower Table movement using Pneumatic Cylidner
§ Upper Table movement using Pneumatic Cylinder
§ Adjustment of the gap of upper mould with 4 x heavy screws – adjustment using motors
§ The press is guided using 4 x LM-60 Ball Bearing with 60 mm diameter
§ Top Heater movement using 1 x High Temperature Pneumatic Cylinder
§ There will be insulation + water cooling in the lower platen
§ Sheet Clamping movement using Pneumatic Cylinders
§ Heating control with settable Zones using touchscreen HMI
§ Heating system, Elements: 500 W each
§ Heating element type: IR Ceramic Heating Elements
§ Heater control is using SSR Control System
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 11

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Process Flow with Images
1. Operator will place the sheet on the machine. The clamping area of the sheet will be in the
thermoforming Press. The frame and the area where the sheet is placed is not very warm and
It will be comfortable for the operator to place the sheet. The frame opening will be about
225mm in front. After that 2 switches have to be pressed by the operator for starting the
forming cycle.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Sheet is clamped tight by pneumatic cylinders
3. As soon as the sheet is clamped the heater which is at the back of the machine will move forward
and heat the sheet for Set Time and Temperature. The temperature of the sheet is measured by
Infrared Sheet Temperature Reader provided in heater body.
4. Heater retracts back
5. Once the heater reaches the back position the top platen which is above the sheet will start moving
down. Also, at the same time the bottom platen which is below the sheet will begin to move up. The
stroke of bottom platen is 100 mm and stroke of the top platen is 250 mm. The bottom platen
movement is by toggle mechanism.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
6. Once the upper platen reaches the sheet level and the bottom platen which carries the mould
reaches the sheet level and touch each other, the sheet is formed by mechnical pressure.
7. After some time needed for cooling the sheet the top platen will move up and bottom platen will
move down.
8. Once the platens reach their home position the clamp frame will open and formed sheet will be
removed by the operator.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 11

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

5 mm (male)

Machine 2: Cutting Machine
Hydraulically operated Cutting machine of area: 300 mm x 700 mm. The machine will have the
pneumatically moving cutting die. The formed piece after sealing will be placed on Cutting die which is
moved pneumatically to cutting press. The cutting die is made from steel Rule.The cutting press has
hydraulically operated top plate on which has cutting fixture fitted. The cutting fixture is made to match the
profile of the part to be cut. The machine is fully guarded and has the light guard in front.
Specifications :
§ Maximum cutting area : 300mm x 700mm.
§ Maximum cutting force : 50 tons ( Hydraulically operated)
§ Stroke of the cutting Platen : 100 mm
§ Cutting die movement : Pneumatic.
§ Power requirement : 10 K.W. Approx

CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
THE UNDERSIGNED:
1. Jupiduu Gmbh a company organized and existing under the laws of Germany, with its
registered office and its official address at Am Freibad 1 | 46499 Hamminkeln, hereby
duly represented by Ken Buschmann (the Disclosing Party); and
2. Formpack a company organized and existing under the laws of INDIA with its
registered office and its official address at 505 PALM SPRINGS MALAD W MUMBAI
400064 INDIA hereby duly represented by RUSHABH DOSHI);
Each hereinafter individually also referred to as a Party and together as the Parties.
WHEREAS:
(A) Jupiduu Gmbh is a company that develops high quality children’s toys that have
been making children’s eyes light up since 2014
(B) FORMPACK is a company that MANUFACTURES Thermoformed parts using plastic
material as a sub-contractor
(C) In order to enable the Parties to jointly explore a possible future cooperation between
them on the field of part development using thermoforming (the Purpose) it is
anticipated that a Party by itself and/or through its Affiliates (as defined in Section 4
hereinafter) (the Disclosing Party) may disclose to the other Party (the Receiving
Party) Confidential Information (as defined in Section 1 hereinafter) and the
Disclosing Party wishes to ensure that such Confidential Information is subject to the
provisions of this confidentiality agreement (the Agreement).
HEREBY AGREES AS FOLLOWS:
1. Information such as CAD data disclosed to the other Party, before or after the date of
entering into this Agreement, whether in writing or in the form of drawings, orally or
electronically or in any other way, is hereinafter referred to as Confidential
Information.
2. The Receiving Party:
i) acknowledges that the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is confidential
and proprietary to the Disclosing Party;
ii) undertakes to maintain the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential
Information;
iii) shall protect the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and any copies made
thereof against disclosure, theft and unauthorized access in the same manner and
with the same degree of care, but not less than a reasonable degree of care, with
which it protects Confidential Information of its own;

[PAGE 2]
iv) shall use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and
for the conduct of consultations and negotiations in relation thereto;
v) shall not exploit or use the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, whether in
modified form or not, for the purpose of developing (derivative) products/services
for itself or third parties;
vi) shall not alter, decompile, disassemble, attempt to decipher or otherwise reverse
engineer any Confidential Information or any part thereof, nor permit others to do
so; and
vii) shall not use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any way
detrimental to the Disclosing Party’s interests.
3. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information
without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent only to the extent that such
information:
i) is already known to the Receiving Party before the date of disclosure under this
Confidentiality Agreement and not subject to any confidentiality obligation
towards the Disclosing Party;
ii) is already in possession of the public or becomes available to the public other than
through the Receiving Party’s breach of this Confidentiality Agreement;
iii) is required to be disclosed under any applicable law, court order or direction, or
governmental or regulatory authority regulation, rule or requirement;
iv) is required to be disclosed by the rules and regulations of any recognized stock
exchange upon which the Receiving Party's or its Affiliates' stock is quoted; or
v) is acquired lawfully from a third party which is not subject to any confidentiality
obligation towards the Disclosing Party;
vi) is developed independently by the Receiving Party without having any knowledge
of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
4. This Agreement shall be effective from 1st April 2023 for a period of [3 (three)] years.
5. Tool Ownership: The ownership of the tool and the associated intellectual properties
belong to Jupiduu GmbH. Any thermoforming production process by using these tools
takes place exclusively for Jupiduu GmbH as per their orders. It will be stored with
utmost security at Formpack’s tool storage unit. Once the order is complete and if
Jupiduu demands for the tool to be sent back to them, then Formpack will do so by
properly packing it
6. Amendments of, and addenda to, this Confidentiality Agreement are only valid if in
writing and signed by the duly authorized representatives of both Parties.
7. This Agreement comprises the full and complete agreement of the Parties with
respect to the subject matter hereof and cancels all prior communications,
understandings and agreements between the Parties relating thereto, whether written
or oral, expressed or implied.

[PAGE 3]
8. If any provision or part of a provision of this Confidentiality Agreement is found by a
court or authority of competent jurisdiction to be void or unenforceable, that provision
or part of a provision shall be deemed to be deleted from this Confidentiality
Agreement and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
AS AGREED UPON IN TWOFOLD AND SIGNED BY:
Jupiduu Gmbh FORMPACK

image ii. preblow control image iii. sag control

to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds

frames

driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time

sheet is loaded

place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads

happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine – so the part is also automatically unloaded from the machine

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine

2. Machine Desciption – Process Wise
I. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system directly via the HMI
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 15

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ There are CNC machined pusher boxes with Linear Rail Guides that move front & back by the
connected servo motor that move the universal frames
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on 2 x sides – left & right & 32 mm
thick Steel Flat part in front & back
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: 50 mm (left & right each side) and 50 mm (front & back each side)
Max. Forming Area mm 2000 x 6500
Min. Forming Area mm 1200 x 5000
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The bottom table will have aluminum plate – CNC machined with channel for moving water inside the
aluminum plate for cooling the tool with contact
§ The base will have grid with M8 blind hole to mount the tool onto the base plate using bolts
§ The aluminium plate can be taken out of the machine to bolt the tool onto the plate - there will be 2 x
rows of ball transfer units
§ The aluminium plate will be clamped on the bottom table using pneumatic system
§ There will be quick connectors to quickly remove the water connection from the aluminum plate
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 15

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
II. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ The base frame on which the pallet is to be loaded comes out of the automatic loader using
pneumatic system – for easy loading of the sheets into the autoloader
§ There will be a sheet centering system – using 2 x servo motors & lead screw system – to centre
the top sheets – in each cycle – while the sheet is being heated for the forming cycle
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side
§ There will be a stack of max. 500 mm in height possible to load in the automatic sheet loader
§ The max. capacity to load the sheets is 7 tonnes
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 15

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Quartz Type
Heater Size mm 250 x 65
Heater Wattage – Top W 500 (each)
Heater Wattage - Bottom W 300 (each)
Number of Heaters - Top 25 rows x 24 columns
Number of Heaters - Bottom 25 rows x 24 columns
Heater Control via SSR – solid state relay with an
Indicator to show if any heater
element is malfunctioning
1 x SSR connected to 2 x heater
element for Individual heater
control & Zone control using the
setting available on HMI
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 15

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Heater Movement Type Servo Motor Driven but also will
have pneumatic cylinders to
retract the heaters in-case of
power failure (for safety)
Sheet Temperature Sensing Using Pyrometer – isolated from
the heater oven using special
construction
c. Sheet Clamping
§ The sheet is clamped tightly on all 4 x sides using strong rack & pinion mechanism
§ There are 4 x racks moving in sync. via 4 x servo motors & gearboxes and vertical travel using
guides from Hiwin
§ There is a pneumatic lock to lock the clamp frame when on the upper position – for safety
§ The motors used are with electrical brakes
d. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 15

[PAGE 8]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the sheet from touhing the bottom heater and also to prevent the sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell – in the upper area - manually height
adjustable for bubble height detection & Timer Control
for blowing air to make the bubble – available on the
touchscreen HMI
ii Sag Control System 3 x Photocells – center & diagonal – approx.. 150 mm
below the sheet level & 50 mm above the bottom heater
e. Table Movements:
§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
i Bottom Table Movement Electric Servo Motor Driven
Electric using 4 x Servo Motors
Driving mechanism – special chain type system that is easy
to maintain & has lower maintenance cost
The table can be stopped at any distance between 0 to max.
stroke – for products with shorter height
The table can have acceleration / deceleration possible for a
soft release of the tool after forming and also a soft touch
town with the heated sheet
Results in faster cycle time
Max. Tool weight: 2000 kg
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 8 of 15

[PAGE 9]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
ii Top Table Movement Using 2 x pneumatic cylinders – height adjustment motorized
using setting available on touchscreen HMI
f. Cooling System:
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
i Cooling System Type Ducted & Centrally connected to a blower to pull air from
the bottom area

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
PF1 Series stands for Polymer Forming machine. Machinecraft displayed the first PF1 series at the K Show
in Dusseldorf in 1998. Machinecraft has built PF1 Series in different sizes and options - has installed them
globally in India, Middle East, Europe and North America. Each machine is custom tailor made as per the
client’s request – size, movements and automation options.
The machine in the photo is just a reference photo of a similar spec “XL” sized machine. The thermoforming
machine is of max. forming area 4.25 x 2 m in this photo.
The standard machine colour is light grey RAL 7035 and black grey RAL 7021 with important machine parts
being painted in signal yellow RAL 1003
Below we have prepared a custom made machine offer as per specs provided by you

forming area to heat the thermoplastic sheet. So you can thermoform plastic sheet upto 10 mm
thickness without any problems.
▪ Independent moving top & bottom heater ovens
▪ The heater ovens have infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature
to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
▪ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
▪ Preblow photocell is above the sheet and sag photocell is below the sheet

4. Sheet Loading, Clamp Frame System & final part unloading:
image v. universal bottom frame image vi. universal top frame

a. Automatic Sheet Loader System
▪ The first step is to load the plastic sheet into the machine – this is done using a special design
automatic sheet loader
▪ The Autoloader robot placed on the right/left side of the machine – as per customer choice.
▪ The autoloader first centers the sheet, then makes sure that only 1 x sheet is lifted each time using a
separator mechanism
▪ The autoloader picks 1 x thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using an up/down
& in/out mechanism which are both servo motor driven
▪ The sheet is cleaned by special micro-cleaning system in each cycle using ionized air
▪ After the forming cycle, the autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
▪ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up
b. Universal Frame System
▪ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system
▪ The operator can enter the sheet size on the HMI and the bottom and top frames – driven by 4 x servo
motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
▪ There will be 3 x sets of universal frames available with the machine to be changed using a forklift to
achieve all possible size reductions
▪ Additional reduction can be possible with additional universal frames

the door is open power supply is cut from the machine to keep it safe

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
8. Vacuuming System
▪ After the sheet reaches the glass transition system, vacuum is applied from the bottom of the vacuum
forming mould.
▪ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
▪ The machine comes with an integrated genereosuly dimensioned vacuum tank in the back side of the
machine connected to the vacuum pump.
▪ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is to
supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for
the pneumatic system.
▪ For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is reduced in a controlled manner
(operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out and the working pressure is monitored.
▪ There is additional servo ball valve from Festo present between vacuum tank and table to allow
proportional vacuuming in the machine – settable on the HMI.

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specifications
Max. Forming Area mm 1500 x 1500
Max. Draw Mm 500
Heater Load KW 76.8
Vacuum Pump Rate m3/hr 100
Total Installed Power KW 80
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 10

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Process Flow
There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader
Sheet Loading Sheet Clamping
Heating
Vacuum Forming
Part Unloading Cooling
3. Machine-Startup
a. Static Type Sheet Size Setting Frames
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 10

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
■ The tool is expected to be loaded using a fork-lift and place the tool on top of the bottom table using
support channels
■ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
■ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – Manual
■ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet into the machine manually and then starts the auto-
cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish
■ Once the part is formed, he takes it out of the machine
b. Heating Ovens
■ The machine comes with double heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto 10
mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
■ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 10

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Ceramic
Heater Size 250 x 95 mm each
Heater Wattage – Top / Bottom 500 W / 300 W each
Number of Heaters 96 approx.
Heater Control Using solid state relay (SSR)
No. of Heater elements looped 2 x heaters connected to 1 x SSR
Heater Movement Type Using High Temp Pneumatic Cyl.
Heater Control Type Zone Control
c. Table Movements:
■ There is 1 x table present on the machine – below the sheet
■ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
■ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
■ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
■ Front Door will be locked using manually adjusted locks – actualted using a hand wrench
i Table Movement Pneumatic Driven using 4 x cylinders
d. Cooling System:
■ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 10

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
i Cooling System Type Centrifugal Fans x 4 pcs
5. Vacuuming System
■ The machine comes with a genereosuly dimensioned vacuum tank
■ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is to
supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for
the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is reduced in a
controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out and the working pressure is
monitored.
■ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
i Vacuum Pump Capacity 100 cubic meter per hour
ii Vacuum Tank Size 1600 lit
6. Electrical Cabinet
■ The machine parameters are set on touchscreen 7’ HMI
■ HMI is in English
■ Software has been developed alongside machine operators over the last 25 years
■ Visualisation of heater bank settings and all other process parameters – I/O status, alarms, etc.
■ Heating controlled as individual or total field adjustment
■ All machine operations are controlled using PLC placed inside an Electrical Cabinet
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 10

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Ability to store recipes in SD card - Recipe and program management
7. Make of Components
■ PLC, HMI & Servos: Mitusbishi / Delta
■ Pneumatics: Festo / SMC / Janatics
■ Light Guard & Sensors: Sick / PnF
■ Vacuum Pump: Zen Air India
■ Heater Elements: Stella
■ SSR: Unison
Note: In-case any item is not available due to abnormal lead time then the item of similar quality / brand will
be used – client will be informed prior
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 10

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

a) Presently approx. 16-20 weeks after technical and commercial clarification depending on the

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specifications
Max. Sheet Dimensions mm 3050 x 3050
Max. Spa Size Mm 2800 x 2800
Max. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 3000 x 3000
Min. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 1500 x 1500
Max. Tool Height in Z direction mm 1000
Heater Load KW 312
Vacuum Pump Rate m3/hr 300
Number of Servo Motors 15
Servo Motor Load KW 49.6
Total Installed Power approx.. KW 365 KW
Air consumption cfm 50 to 100
www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 13

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Process Flow
There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader
Sheet Loading Sheet Clamping Heating
Vacuum Forming
Part Unloading Cooling
3. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system – without any manual work
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature control
§ The top frame also has copper pipes embedded in front & back and also on the cross member
frame. The cross-member will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet.
§ MTC with chiller has to be connected to the copper pipes
§ There will also be small air jet nozzles in the upper frame for cooling the frame after the heating
www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 13

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on all 2 x sides
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: max. 25 mm (left & right) and max. 40 mm (front & back)
Max. Forming Area mm 3000 x 3000
Min. Forming Area mm 1500 x 1500
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§ For tool loading – the tool must be loaded on the machine using a fork-lift from the front access doors
§ The bottom table will have aluminum plate – CNC machined with groove lines to add rubbber (New!)
§ The tool is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table using pneumatic
system – 2 x hollow cylidners that lift the tool and 1 x locking cylinder that locks the tool – so the tool is
always aligned in the centre & is a quick way to clamp the tool
§ An operator has to use hand-valve to twist to unlock / lock a tool using the special pneumatic system
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 13

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side
§ There will be a special Jet of Air with ionized particles for cleaning the A-surface of the sheet
between the autoloader & the main forming station
4.1 Sheet Loading Using Automatic Sheet Loader using 2 x servo motors
4.2 Sheet Cleaning Using Jet of ionized air
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
12 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
§ Both heaters (upper/lower) move along independent linear guides
www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 13

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ Lower Heater is protected by special ceramic glass
§ The sheet size can be seen as a visual on the heater setup screen
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Quartz Type
Heater Size mm 250 x 95
Heater Wattage – Top KW 172
Heater Wattage - Bottom W 138
Heater Control via SSR – solid state relay
1 x SSR connected to 2 x heater
element for Individual heater
control & Zone control using the
setting available on HMI
Heater Movement Type Servo Motor Driven but also will
have pneumatic cylinders to
retract the heaters in-case of
power failure (for safety)
Sheet Temperature Sensing Using Pyrometer
c. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
ii Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center
www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 13

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
d. Table Movements:
§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is usually loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
i Bottom Table Movement Electric Servo Motor Driven
Electric using 4 x Servo Motors
Driving mechanism – special chain type system that is easy
to maintain & has lower maintenance cost
The table can be stopped at any distance between 0 to max.
stroke – for products with shorter height
The table can have acceleration / deceleration possible for a
soft release of the tool after forming and also a soft touch
town with the heated sheet
Results in faster cycle time
Max. Tool weight: 1500 kg
ii Plug Assist Using 2 x pneumatic cylinders – height adjustment motorized
using setting available on touchscreen HMI – clamping of
plug assist to be done using bolts
www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 13

[PAGE 8]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
e. Cooling System:
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
i Cooling System Type Ducted & Centrally connected to a blower to pull air from

in Dusseldorf in 1998. Machinecraft has installed them globally in India, Middle East, Europe and North
America. Each machine is custom tailor made as per the client’s request – size, movements and
automation options.

mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time

components that are available in your region

■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,

homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

touchscreen HMI

2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance.
■ AMC Series Machine – with max. forming 700 x 1500 mm and max. product depth 150 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1". The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox.
The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Dual/Sandwich Heating System with Top and Bottom heaters. Heaters can be moved
into the forming station so that they are as near the mould as possible. This is important for
uniformity in heating of the sheet to be formed.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements are European make
■ Heater Oven length is 1500 mm
Quote no.: 2023097 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 9

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped using a spring loaded clamping system
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
Quote no.: 2023097 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 9

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter – cuts each shot from the machine to be then individually cut using a steel
rule die on a hydraulic press
■ Trim Winding
■ The trim-winding Station winds the left-over plastic sheet trim after punching. The winder is driven
by a geared motor of 1/2 HP
■ Vacuum System & Cooling
■ Water Mist based cooling after the forming
■ 100 m3/hr vacuum pump with tank
■ Vacuum Pump from Becker Germany
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC control
■ HMI: 7" HMI color touchscreen
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI
Optional QUICK Tool Change System
■ Tool Change System – we will provide a tool change trolley with the machine with roller ball bearings on
the platoform to allow sliding of the tool – there will be pneumatic cylinders below the bearings so that
when the tool needs sliding they lift the bearings. The tool can be changed from the side area.
■ Tool Clamping will happen using quick tool clamping cylinders & knobs in the forming press.

mechanical components

appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements

frames

driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

fitted with IR quartz heater elements, machine movements – electric servo motor driven, vacuum pump +
tank & waterlines for both tables, optional trimming table upper for trimming – all process parameters
settable on touch-screen HMI and machine controlled using PLC system.

§ Once the sheet is loaded, it is then clamped tightly using 2 x pneumatic cylinders and a clamp frame
§ The clamp frame is steel welded – of the size of the sheet with rubber groove to add rubber for sealing
§ There are 2 x frames for sheet size setting on the machine – the bottom frame is aluminum CNC
machined frame and the Top Frame comes with adjustable members
5. Heater System:
§ The most important objective of a thermoforming machine is the heating of the thermoplastic sheet
homogeneously – this is possible with zone heating
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets from
both sides – the upper side of the foil is the A-side & lower side is the B-side
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heater movement is servo motor driven (top & bottom) – the heaters come in front ontop of the
forming area to heat the sheet
§ In-case of power failure there is an air-safety tank that uses the pneumatic cylinders to takes the heater
to the home position (for safety)

§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” using pulsated air is possible in the machine
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging

forming area

Process Flow with Images
1. Operator will place the sheet on the machine. The clamping area of the sheet will be in the
thermoforming Press. The frame and the area where the sheet is placed is not very warm and
It will be comfortable for the operator to place the sheet. The frame opening will be about
225mm in front. After that 2 switches have to be pressed by the operator for starting the
forming cycle.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Sheet is clamped tight by pneumatic cylinders
3. As soon as the sheet is clamped the heater which is at the back of the machine will move forward
and heat the sheet for Set Time and Temperature. The temperature of the sheet is measured by
Infrared Sheet Temperature Reader provided in heater body.
4. Heater retracts back
5. Once the heater reaches the back position the top platen which is above the sheet will start moving
down. Also, at the same time the bottom platen which is below the sheet will begin to move up. The
stroke of bottom platen is 100 mm and stroke of the top platen is 250 mm. The bottom platen
movement is by toggle mechanism.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
6. Once the upper platen reaches the sheet level and the bottom platen which carries the mould
reaches the sheet level and touch each other, the sheet is formed by mechnical pressure.
7. After some time needed for cooling the sheet the top platen will move up and bottom platen will
move down.
8. Once the platens reach their home position the clamp frame will open and formed sheet will be
removed by the operator.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 11

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
PF1 Series stands for Polymer Forming machine. Machinecraft displayed the first PF1 series at the K Show
in Dusseldorf in 1998. Machinecraft has installed the PF1 Series globally in India, Middle East, Europe and
North America. Each machine is custom tailor made as per the client’s request – size, movements and
automation options.
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specs for PF1-1210
1.1 Max. Forming Area 1000 x 1200 mm
1.2 Max Tool Depth in Z Direction 300 mm
1.3 Total Heater Load 62.5 KW appox.
1.4 Vacuum Pump Capacity 1000 lpm
Quote no.: 2022121602 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 10

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Heater System:
image i. IR heater oven - zone control heating
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
10 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI
2.1 Heater Type IR Ceramic
2.2 Heater Size 245 x 63 mm
2.3 Heater Wattage 500 W each (Top)
350 W each (Bottom)
2.4 Heater Control System Solid State Relay (SSR)
2.5 Heater Movement Type Pneumatic Driven using High
Temperature Cylinders
2.6 Heater Grid Layout 5 Rows x 10 Columns in Top & Bottom
Ovens (approx..)
2.7 Heater Control Individual (in pairs) or Zone Control
using setting available on HMI
2.8 Heater Protection Sag Sensor in the bottom to protect
the bottom heater oven
2.9 IR Pyrometer 1 x Pyrometer from Raytec to measure
the actual temperature of the sheet in
the centre of top heater oven – to
make heating closed loop control
Quote no.: 2022121602 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 10

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
3. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
image ii. preblow & sag control using light
sensor for even sheet thickness
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” using pulsated air is possible in the machine
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
3.1 Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
3.2 Sag Control Syste 1 x Photocell – center
4. Sheet Size Setting System & Sheet Loading:
image iii. Bottom frame steel welded & Upper frame adjustable cross members
§ The sheet is loaded manually by a operator
§ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
§ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
Quote no.: 2022121602 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 10

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
§ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included
4.1 Sheet Loading Manual Sheet Loading by Operator
4.2 Sheet Clamping Using 2 x Pneumatic Cylinders
4.3 Bottom Frame Adjustment Steel Welded Fixed Type
4.4 Top Frame Adjustment Adjustable Cross-Members
5. Mould Table, Clamping System & Plug Assist:
image iv. Bottom Table & Top Table – Pneumatic Driven
Image v. connection of ancilliary equipment –
MTC with water connection
§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is usually loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
§ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the bottom table - M10 bolts with blind holes
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
Quote no.: 2022121602 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 10

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
§ There will be special temperature controller to allow machine to measure tool temperature using
contact heaters
5.1 Bottom Table Movement Pneumatic Driven
Using 4 x cylinders working in sync using rack &
pinion mechanism
5.2 Tool Clamping Using bolts on the lower platen
5.3 Top Table Movement Using 1 x pneumatic cylinder – manually
adjusted height
5.4 Plug Asist Clamping Using bolts on the upper platen

image xii. cooling fans
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it

§ The machine comes with an integrated genereosuly dimensioned vacuum tank
§ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is to
supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for
the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is reduced in a
controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out and the working pressure is
monitored.

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
7.1 Vacuum Pump Capacity 1000 lpm

image xiv. touchscreen HMI operation image xv. FRL system & electrical panel

tool is clamped

clamped using bolts by the operator on all 4 x sides - once he clamps the tool, the operator presses a
button to indicate that the tool is clamped

[PAGE 2]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Machine 1: Press Machine
There will be a press to give shape to the bio-cellulose material using match mould.
§ Basic Mild Steel welded machine frame – compact design
§ Electrical system – PLC control
§ Control panel 10" touchscreen (HMI) with SD Card to save Recipes
§ Electrical Cabinet - as per CE guidelines
§ The press is composed of 2 x tables – lower & upper
§ Lower Table movement using Pneumatic Cylidner
§ Upper Table movement using Pneumatic Cylinder
§ Adjustment of the gap of upper mould with 4 x heavy screws – adjustment using motors
§ The press is guided using 4 x LM-60 Ball Bearing with 60 mm diameter
§ Top Heater movement using 1 x High Temperature Pneumatic Cylinder
§ There will be insulation + water cooling in the lower platen
§ Sheet Clamping movement using Pneumatic Cylinders
§ Heating control with settable Zones using touchscreen HMI
§ Heating system, Elements: 500 W each
§ Heating element type: IR Ceramic Heating Elements
§ Heater control is using SSR Control System
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 11

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Process Flow with Images
1. Operator will place the sheet on the machine. The clamping area of the sheet will be in the
thermoforming Press. The frame and the area where the sheet is placed is not very warm and
It will be comfortable for the operator to place the sheet. The frame opening will be about
225mm in front. After that 2 switches have to be pressed by the operator for starting the
forming cycle.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Sheet is clamped tight by pneumatic cylinders
3. As soon as the sheet is clamped the heater which is at the back of the machine will move forward
and heat the sheet for Set Time and Temperature. The temperature of the sheet is measured by
Infrared Sheet Temperature Reader provided in heater body.
4. Heater retracts back
5. Once the heater reaches the back position the top platen which is above the sheet will start moving
down. Also, at the same time the bottom platen which is below the sheet will begin to move up. The
stroke of bottom platen is 100 mm and stroke of the top platen is 250 mm. The bottom platen
movement is by toggle mechanism.
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
6. Once the upper platen reaches the sheet level and the bottom platen which carries the mould
reaches the sheet level and touch each other, the sheet is formed by mechnical pressure.
7. After some time needed for cooling the sheet the top platen will move up and bottom platen will
move down.
8. Once the platens reach their home position the clamp frame will open and formed sheet will be
removed by the operator.

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specifications
Max. Sheet Dimensions mm 2550 x 2550
Max. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 2500 x 2500
Min. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 1250 x 1250
Max. Tool Height in Z direction mm 100
Heater Load KW 216
Vacuum Pump Rate m3/hr 300
Number of Servo Motors 15
Servo Motor Load KW 49.6
Total Installed Power approx.. KW 272 KW
Air consumption cfm 50 to 100
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 13

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Process Flow
There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader
Sheet Loading Sheet Clamping Heating
Vacuum Forming
Part Unloading Cooling
3. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system – without any manual work
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature control
§ The top frame also has copper pipes embedded in front & back and also on the cross member
frame. The cross-member will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet.
§ MTC with chiller has to be connected to the copper pipes
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 13

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ There will also be small air jet nozzles in the upper frame for cooling the frame after the heating
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on all 2 x sides
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: max. 25 mm (left & right) and max. 40 mm (front & back)
Max. Forming Area mm 2500 x 2500
Min. Forming Area mm 1250 x 1250
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§ For tool loading – the tool must be loaded on the machine using a fork-lift from the front access doors
§ The bottom table will have aluminum plate – CNC machined with groove lines to add rubbber (New!)
§ The tool is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table using pneumatic
system – 2 x hollow cylidners that lift the tool and 1 x locking cylinder that locks the tool – so the tool is
always aligned in the centre & is a quick way to clamp the tool
§ An operator has to use hand-valve to twist to unlock / lock a tool using the special pneumatic system
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 13

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
12 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
§ Both heaters (upper/lower) move along independent linear guides
§ Lower Heater is protected by special ceramic glass
§ The sheet size can be seen as a visual on the heater setup screen
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Quartz Type
Heater Size mm 250 x 95
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 13

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
Heater Wattage – Top KW 120
Heater Wattage - Bottom W 96
Heater Control via SSR – solid state relay
1 x SSR connected to 2 x heater
element for Individual heater
control & Zone control using the
setting available on HMI
Heater Movement Type Servo Motor Driven but also will
have pneumatic cylinders to
retract the heaters in-case of
power failure (for safety)
Sheet Temperature Sensing Using Pyrometer
c. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
ii Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 13

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
d. Table Movements:
§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is usually loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
i Bottom Table Movement Electric Servo Motor Driven
Electric using 4 x Servo Motors
Driving mechanism – special chain type system that is easy
to maintain & has lower maintenance cost
The table can be stopped at any distance between 0 to max.
stroke – for products with shorter height
The table can have acceleration / deceleration possible for a
soft release of the tool after forming and also a soft touch
town with the heated sheet
Results in faster cycle time
Max. Tool weight: 1500 kg
ii Plug Assist Using 2 x pneumatic cylinders – height adjustment motorized
using setting available on touchscreen HMI – clamping of
plug assist to be done using bolts
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 13

[PAGE 8]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
e. Cooling System:
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
i Cooling System Type Ducted & Centrally connected to a blower to pull air from
the bottom area
ii Sheet Tmperature Sensing (Cooling) 1 x Pyrometer to measure final temperature of the cooled
part
5. Vacuuming System
§ The machine comes

automation options.

4. Automatic Sheet Loading
image iii. Automatic sheet loader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system to make sure only 1 x sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table
§ There will be sheet centering system in the automatic sheet loader
4.1 Sheet Loading Using Automatic Sheet Loader using 2 x servo motors
4.2 Sheet Centering Using 2 x Servo Motors

3. Machine-Startup
a. Static Type Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
§ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
§ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
§ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The tool is expected to be loaded using a fork-lift and place the tool on top of the bottom table using
support channels
§ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
§ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides

cycle

b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with single heater oven in top – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto 4 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI

special

completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds

with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC

being either in the bottom or top. The height of the sensor in

4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side

from all sides [40 x 5mm thick angles].

plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part

as standard
