[PAGE 2]
1. Quote Basis
1.1 Your scope of work
Your inquiry FCS machine to make blisters & clamshell with 0.15 mm PVC & PET sheets
Customer specifications FCS 500 x 650 mm
1.2 Machine layout
FCS stands for Form-Cut-Stack machine type – multistation thermoforming machine – there are 4 stations
– stations being heating, forming, holing, punching and stacking. The machine is fully servo driven and runs
at dry cycle speed of upto 30 cycles per minute.
FCS Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The FCS Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 11

[PAGE 3]
2. Machinery Description
■ FCS Machine – with forming area 500 x 650 mm and max. product depth 140 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine. This loop is sensed by a cat-whiskers type limit switch
to stop the motor. The mechanical limit switch is offered as it is able to sense transparent plastic
materials too. The unwinder can hold sheet roll of max. width 650 mm and weight 400 kgs.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox. The film from the in-feed is transported into different
stations by the spike chain accurately using a servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/-
0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Dual/Sandwich Heating System with Top and Bottom heaters. Heaters can be moved
into the forming station so that they are as near the mould as possible. This is important for
uniformity in heating of the sheet to be formed.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements from Elstein Germany
■ Heater Elements are placed in 6 rows and 8 columns i.e 48 heating elements in top & bottom each
■ Top Heater: 500 W each, Bottom heater: 350 W each
■ Total Heating Load: 40.8 KW
■ Forming Station (Station 1)
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using servo motors
■ There will be a helping frame included in the forming station
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
■ The forming press has a pressure box in top/bottom connected to a tank that generates pressure
upto 4 bar. The pressure can be adjusted using pressure regulator
■ The air release is regulated using a pneumatic cylinder controlled as per setting done on the HMI
■ Holing Station (Station 2)
■ The hole cutting die is mounted on the holing platen
■ The holing platens are driven up/down by the holing press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ Punching Station (Station 3)
■ The steel ruled cutting die is mounted on the punching platen
■ The punching platens are driven up/down by the punching press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ The punching force of upto 60 tonnes is generated by the toggle design
■ There is a chain mechanism for height adjustment in the punching press
■ For fine aligning of the punching tool, there is a special xy mechanism on linear guide
■ The XY setting is motorized using hand knobs
■ Temperature controller and provision will be there in puching station so that you can have heated
punching tool ideal for PET cutting
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 11

[PAGE 6]
■ The holing and punching station distances are adjustable and driven using servo motors – hence this
too can be stored in the form of receipe as per the product
■ The lubrication in the presses is automatic
■ Stacking Station (Station 4)
■ Up Stacking using Pneumatic Motion
■ Operator can set a fixed number of items and the pusher will then put them up before stacking more
below – double layer
■ There will be pneumatic push out with coveyor present in the machine
■ Trim Winding
■ The trim-winding Station winds the left-over plastic sheet trim after punching
■ The winder is driven by a geared motor of 1/2 HP
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 10" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI box is on a swivel mechanism
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 11

The same tool will have 2 x height adjustable plates – to have heights of 40 mm, 50 mm and 70 mm

■ Basic Technical Specs

■ Chain Indexing
■ RM is in roll, it can be placed on the roll un-winder and then loaded onto the chain
■ The in-feed area will have sheet edge heaters fitted, each a seprate zone
■ The material is indexed in spiked chain of 1/2" duplex type with spike repeating every 1", the spike
will piere thin sheet & for thick sheet spike will touch the surface to index the sheet
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox
■ The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the
machine
■ The chain rails are water cooled
■ Heating Oven
■ The sheet first enters the heating station which is roll out design
■ The oven will fit between platens for short tool lengths
■ Machine has Single Heater Oven in Top – moved using high temperature pneumatic cylinder
■ Heaters can be setup in Zones using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be
modified individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements

■ There are 9 x 8 = 72 heater elements in the heating oven
■ The heaters are controlled using SSR which are controlled using the PLC system & PID logic
■ There is a sag sensor at the bottom of the sheet to detect excessive sagging and the chain indexing
stops and a notification alarm pops up on the HMI for the operator to stop the machine and restart
■ There will be a pyrometer in the heating oven which will indicate the true temperature of the sheet
on the HMI and prevents servo plug running when sheet temperature is below a defined set point
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped from top & bottom

■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen

■ Bottom platen: 100mm stroke

■ The punching platens are driven up/down by the punching press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ The punching force of upto 90 tonnes is generated by the toggle design

to supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air

supply for the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

1. Technical Specifications
Max. Forming Area mm 1500 x 2500
Max. Draw Mm 750

2. Process Flow
There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader
Sheet Loading Sheet Clamping
Heating
Vacuum Forming
Part Unloading Cooling
3. Machine-Startup
a. Static Type Sheet Size Setting Frames
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 10

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
■ The tool is expected to be loaded using a fork-lift and place the tool on top of the bottom table using
support channels
■ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
■ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – Manual
■ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet into the machine manually and then starts the auto-
cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish
■ Once the part is formed, he takes it out of the machine
b. Heating Ovens
■ The machine comes with double heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto 10
mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
■ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 10

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

No. of Heater elements looped 2 x heaters connected to 1 x SSR
Heater Movement Type Using High Temp Pneumatic Cyl.
Heater Control Type Zone Control
c. Table Movements:
■ There is 1 x table present on the machine – below the sheet
■ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
■ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
■ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
■ Front Door will be locked using manually adjusted locks – actualted using a hand wrench
i Table Movement Pneumatic Driven using 4 x cylinders
d. Cooling System:

■ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is to
supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for
the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is reduced in a
controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out and the working pressure is
monitored.
■ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
i Vacuum Pump Capacity 140 cubic meter per hour
ii Vacuum Tank Size 1600 lit

a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
Quote no.: MT2023190 www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 22

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded

place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads

happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine
§ There will be sheet centering system in the automatic sheet loader
b. Heating Ovens

homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

Description:
● 9 KW air cooling spindle powerful enough for cutting a variety kind of plastic, ABS, PP,
PE, PVC, PS, etc. Spindle speed range from 0 to 18000r/min. This spindle can keep
powerful cutting even at low speed
● Servo motor. The integrated high-performance servo drive adopts advanced control
algorithm which can drive the motor at very high precision smoothly with very little noise
and motor heating. In addition, outputs of fault and “in position” signals in a integrated
servo motor can be directly sent to the motion controller
● Taiwan linear square lead rail, high positioning accuracy, high repeatability. The frictional
resistance of a linear guideway is only 1/20th to 1/40th of that in a slide guide. The
required driving force is much lower than in other systems, thus the power consumption
of machine is small. Moreover, the temperature rising effect is small even under high
speed operation.

1. Quote Basis
1.1 Your scope of work
Machine to produce 3D car mats with TPE material - 2 mm max. thickness roll on a continuous type
vacuum forming machine
1.2 Machine layout
AMC Series is a large format roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the
sheet from heating to forming and to the shot cutting station
Sheet Sheet Vacuum Shot
Heating Cooling
unwinding Clamping Forming Cutting
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 9

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance.
■ AMC Series Machine – with max. forming 700 x 1500 mm and max. product depth 150 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine.
■ Chain Indexing
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section
■ The material is indexed in spiked chain of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox
■ The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the
machine
■ Heating Ovens
■ The sheet first enters the heating station
■ Machine has Single Heater Oven in Top – moved using high temperature pneumatic cylinder
■ Heaters can be setup in Zones using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be
modified individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements are European make
■ Heater element size is 265 x 95 mm
■ Heater wattage is 500 W each
■ There are 8 x 8 = 64 heater elements in the heating oven
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 9

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ The heaters are controlled using SSR which are controlled using the PLC system & PID logic
■ There is a sag sensor at the bottom of the sheet to detect excessive sagging and the chain indexing
stops and a notification alarm pops up on the HMI for the operator to stop the machine and restart
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped using a spring loaded clamping system
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 9

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

■ There are 2 x centrifugal fans with water mist present in the forming station for fast cooling after the
forming
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter – cuts each shot from the machine
■ Vacuum System
■ 100 m3/hr vacuum pump with tank
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 10" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI

forming area

ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)

support channels

homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

touchscreen HMI

to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging

a. Universal Sheet Size Setting System
▪ The Universal Sheet Size System – allows the operator to set the sheet size automatically
▪ The operator can enter the sheet size on the HMI and the bottom and top frames – driven by 4 x servo
motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top adjust automatically
▪ The max. sheet size can be “1050 x 1850” mm and min forming area can be “550 x 850” mm
b. Autoloader
▪ The first step is to load the plastic sheet into the machine – this is done automatically by the Autoloader
- to reduce the opeartors work – this is an automated solution.
▪ The Autoloader robot placed on the right/left side of the machine – as per customer choice.
▪ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area.
▪ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
▪ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up

• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region

b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping

for the bolt base

be a metal insert so that the aluminum is not damaged
§ There will be a trolley given with the machine – 2 x members that lock onto the machine – exactly the
level of the tool table with ball transfer units

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
II. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ The pallet height assumed will be 150 mm and then there will be 500 mm stack for sheets
§ The max. capacity to load the sheets is 7 tonnes approx.
§ The base frame on which the pallet is to be loaded comes out of the automatic loader using
pneumatic system – for easy loading of the sheets into the autoloader
§ There will be a sheet centering system – using 2 x servo motors & lead screw system – to centre
the top sheets – in each cycle – while the sheet is being heated for the forming cycle
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
Quote no.: MT2023111 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 16

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heaters are servo motor driven but there will be pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of
power failure for 2-level safety

d. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the sheet from touhing the bottom heater and also to prevent the sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet

1.Project and supply bases
1.1 Your task
You have requested a vacuum forming machine capable of forming bath products of max.
size 2000 mm x 2000 mm and 500 mm depth. The max. sheet thickness to be used on the
machine Is upto 10 mm.
1.2 Our proposed solution
Machinecraft's PF1 Series has been In the market since 1990 and has been continually updated
with new features demanded by the customers - the latest feature being universal frames for
sheet size setting and automatic sheet loader system - which were Introduced at the K2019
Show In Germany In October 2019. Machinecraft has established a technical collaboration
with company FRIMO from Germany In 2019 to further develop the PF1 Series. The PF1 Series
Is a closed chamber type single station thermoforming machine with sandwitch heaters that
heat the thermoplastic sheet as per the profile set on the touchscreen HMI followed by "pre-
blowing" and vacuum process using the tool on the bottom and/or top tables. The machine Is
capable of processing all thermoplastic materials like ABS, PP, PC, TPO, HDPE, HIPS, etc.

■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)

support channels

■ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides

cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish

mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
■ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI

Thermoforming Machine PF1-Twin
§ Basic Mild Steel welded machine frame – compact design – twin station
§ Electrical system – PLC control – make Mitsubishi
§ Control panel touchscreen (HMI)
§ Electrical Cabinet built as per European standards
Quote no.: 2202061301 www.machinecraft.org page 1 of 6

[PAGE 2]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The machine is composed of 2 x table – lower & upper
§ Lower Table movement using Pneumatic Cylidner – to hold the main vacuum forming mould
§ Upper Table movement using Pneumatic Cylinder – to press against the preblow box to allow pre-
blowing bubble
§ There will be 2 x heater banks in top & bottom – sandwitch heating
§ Heater oven is static – the sheet goes inside the oven to be heated
§ Sag control when sheet is being heated is not present – hence thermoplastic like ABS can be
thermoformed on this type of machine
§ Sheet Clamping movement using Pneumatic Cylinders
§ Heating element type: IR Ceramic Heating Elements, Make: European
§ Heater control is using SSR Control System – SSR Make: Unison
Process Flow
1. Operator will place the sheet on the machine. The clamping area of the sheet will be in the
thermoforming machine. After that 2 switches have to be pressed by the operator for starting
the forming cycle.
2. Sheet is clamped tight by pneumatic cylinders
3. As soon as the sheet is clamped the heater – it goes inside the heater oven – in the middle of
the machine and the sheet is heated for Set Time and Temperature.
4. Sheet can sag freely when heated and is not controlled during the heating cycle
5. Once the sheet is heated to the set tempearture, the sheet clamp frames comes back in front
area – the upper table comes down to press against the preblow box – to allow pre-blowing of
the heated sheet
6. After some time the sheet is cooled using centrifugal blowers

Air Circulating Oven from Machinecraft
AO Ovens from Machinecraft are a range of latest generation heating ovens required for pre-drying the
sheet to make it moisture free before starting the thermoforming process.
1. Technical Specs for AO-1015
1.1 Max. Sheet Size 1550 x 2550 mm
1.2 Total Connected Load 35 KW approx
1.3 Temperature Rnage Ambient to 150 deg Celcius
1.4 Heating SS Tubular heaters with fans
1.5 Hot Air Circulation Motorised Air Circulation From Top.
1.6 Exhaust Adjustable Exhaust At Top
1.7 Controller Automatic Electric Digital Temperature
Controller with Timer Facility
Quote no.: MT2021122103 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 4

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Basic Description
■ All Outer and Internal Parts of Oven will be fabricated from CRCA Sheet 18 Swg material and angles
from all sides [40 x 5mm thick angles].
■ Glass wool and Ceramic wool is used as insulation having capacity upto 300 Degree Celsius covering
100mm from all sides. This arrangement ensures efficient heating and cooling of the Oven and also
keeps the casing temperature within permissible limits and also saves energy upto 25%.
■ The Oven is heated electrically by specially designed S.S.Tubular Type Heater with a large surface
area to prevent burn out.
■ The heaters are rewireable type and are accessible for service, inspection and replacement from inside
the Oven.
■ S.S. Tubular heater of 1 KW each with Fins used as heating element. Forced Air Circulation inside the
Oven is provided by three axial flow or centrifugal blower fans.
■ Arrangement for recirculation, supply of fresh air and discharge of vapours is provided.
■ The Air distribution system inside the Oven is designed to suit the application and the nature of product.
Ball Catch Type or Special Locking Arrangement depending upon application.
■ Outside Silver Hammertone Paint Prior treated with Red Oxide and inside Silver Aluminium Paint for
temperature upto 300 Degreee Celsius.
■ CONTROL PANEL

RT Routers from Machinecraft are a range of latest generation 5-axis interpolating machining centres for
trimming thermoformed parts - created to perform at high speed on the most complex 3D shapes. A
compact design with a large machining volume together with high precision and reliability make it the ideal

1. Technical Specs for RT-5A-2618
1.1 Max. Working Area 2600 x 1800 mm
1.2 Max. Travel in Z 1100 mm
1.2 Max. Operating Part (5-Axis Mode) 2000 x 1200 x 800 mm
1.3 Total Connected Load 33.8 KW
1.4 Machine Weight 16,000 kg
2. Basic Description
§ RT-5A is a non-metal five-axis machining center is a truss-type double worktable with a guard
room structure to ensure the best processing dynamic characteristics for machine.
§ During processing, the workpiece table does not move, and the five coordinate axes drive the
tool to move and process around the workpiece, which can realize five-axis linkage three-
dimensional movement. Satisfy the processing of pentahedron and arbitrary curved surface.
§ The workbench does not participate in the processing linkage, but is only responsible for the
transportation of the products to realize the automatic interaction between the processing area
and the loading and unloading area.
§ The truss-type double worktable and the protective room structure meet the safety protection
and debris prevention during production; the floor drain dust collection device cooperates with
the high-performance dust collector to collect processing debris
§ Structural composition: X-axis, Y-axis (dual drive), Z-axis, C-axis, B-axis, working spindle, rotary
table, protective top cover (The general structure refers to the figure below to make minor
adjustments)
3. Detailed Description
Quote no.: MT2022110202 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 8

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
a. Machine Structure
§ The welded steel bed is adopted, the bed is solid, and the key parts are added with ribbed plates.
The main material is Q235 rectangular tube, the wall thickness is 8~10mm, and the thickness of the
connecting plate is not less than 20mm.
§ The classic truss structure, the machine has a solid structure, good precision and reliability, high
safety, meets the requirements of environmental protection work, and is suitable for milling, window
opening, contour processing, finishing, drilling and 3D machining for any non-metal material.
§ After stable tempering and aging treatment, it has good rigidity and static characteristics, which
provide a good guarantee for the high-speed movement of the machine tool.
§ Milling, drilling, and tapping are all completed by CNC to ensure the accuracy of the bed.
b. Transmission parts
§ Hiwin rail guides
§ XY axis adopts Taiwan YYC grinding rack & pinons
§ Z axis adopts Taiwan TBI screw rod
§ XY axis adopts Japanese Shimpo reducer
§ C/B axis adopts Japanese Sumitomo reducer
c. Control System
§ The control system adopts SYNTEC five-axis linkage CNC system, which has great advantages in
terms of function, stability and after-sales service
§ Support five-axis machining;
§ NURBS interpolation function to realize curve motion interpolation
§ HPCC high-speed high-precision contour control
§ The operation and application functions are easy to learn
§ Processing preparation, trial processing, processing monitoring screen, clear interface, easy to
learn and understand.
§ The movable console is equipped with a wireless multi-function hand-held button board, which
is convenient and flexible to operate.
d. Double Swing Head
§ The milling head has 2 CNC continuous rotary coordinates: the 4th (C) axis and the 5th (B) axis,
realizing linkage processing (including milling and hole rolling functions), 3+2 directional processing
origin following (RTCP function) ;
§ The two rotary coordinates are driven by AC servo motors, equipped with high-precision
deceleration devices;
§ The electric spindle adopts special ceramic bearings, lubricated with special grease, no oil leakage,
suitable for high-speed processing;
§ The electric spindle adopts a liquid cooling system to ensure continuous 24-hour processing;
§ The C/B axis adopts ultra-high-precision reducer, which has the characteristics of small size and
large torque.
§ The large rotation angle of the C-axis simplifies the programming process and avoids repeated tool
lifting.

§ Welded steel structure + aluminum plate is adopted, and the main structure of the steel

ВАКУУМНО- ФОРМОВОЧНАЯ МАШИНА серии VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
PF1
PF1 Series stands for Polymer Forming machine.
Серия PF1 означает машины для формования Machinecraft displayed the first PF1 series at the
полимеров. Machinecraft представила первую K Show in Dusseldorf in 1998. Machinecraft has
серию PF1 на K Show в Дюссельдорфе в 1998 installed the PF1 Series globally in India, Middle
году. Machinecraft установила серию PF1 по East, Europe and North America. Each machine is
всему миру в Индии, на Ближнем Востоке, в custom tailor made as per the client’s request –
Европе и Северной Америке. Каждая машина size, movements and automation options
изготавливается на заказ в соответствии с
запросом клиента - размер, движения и
варианты автоматизации.

[PAGE 2]
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine ///
Часть А: Техническое описание машины
1. Technical Specs for PF1-2116
1.1 Max. Forming Area 2100 x 1600 mm
1.2 Min. Forming Area 1200 x 630 mm (will try for min. 620 mm)
1.3 Max Tool Depth in Z Direction 860 mm (Negative)
660 mm (Positive)
1.4 Total Heater Load 288 KW appox.
Total Servo Load 36.9 KW approx.
Total Connected Load 330 KW approx.
1.5 Vacuum Pump Capacity 200 m3/hr
1.6 Approx. Machine Foot Print (L x D x H) 8.5 x 5.5 x 5.5 m approx.
2. Heater System:
image i. IR halogen heater oven – with glass
protection in bottom - individual & zone control
heating & pyrometer

[PAGE 3]
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
10 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
2.1 Heater Type IR Halogen
Fast Response time – so the heater
oven can be switched off when in back
position – resulting in upto 50% energy
saving
2.2 Heater Size Ø10 x 473 mm (Long) & Ø10 x 224 mm
(Short)
2.3 Heater Wattage 1500 W – 90 pcs & 750 W – 12 pcs
(Top) &
1500 W – 90 pcs & 750 W – 12 pcs
(Bottom)
2.4 Heater Control System Special Heatronik Heater controller
2.5 Heater Movement Type Electro-Servo Motor Driven with
pneumatic cylinder for back-up
2.6 No. of Heater Elements 102 Heating elements in Top &
102 Heating elements in Bottom Oven
(approx..)
2.7 Sheet Temperature Sensing 1 x Pyrometer placed in centre of the
heating oven
2.8 Heater Control Individual (in pairs) or Zone Control
using setting available on HMI
2.9 Heater Protection Sag Sensor in the bottom to protect the
bottom heater oven & schott glass for
bottom heater protection
3. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
image ii. preblow & sag control using light
sensor for even sheet thickness
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is

[PAGE 4]
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” using pulsated air is possible in the machine
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
3.1 Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
3.2 Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center
4. Automatic Sheet Loading
image iii. Automatic sheet loader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system to make sure only 1 x sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table
§ There will be a special Jet of Air with ionized particles for cleaning the A-surface of the sheet
between the autoloader & the main forming station
§ There will be sheet centering system in the automatic sheet loader
4.1 Sheet Loading Using Automatic Sheet Loader using 2 x servo motors
4.2 Sheet Centering Using 2 x Servo Motors
4.3 Sheet Cleaning Using Jet of ionized air

[PAGE 5]
5. Universal Frames with Temerature Control
image iv. Bottom frame steel welded & Upper frame motorised – temperature controlled – with air jet system
• The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes –
this is done using a universal sheet size setting system
• The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete
automatic
• The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the
bottom and top frames – driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo
motors in top – adjust automatically
• The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined
• The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature
control
• The top frame also has copper pipes embedded in front & back and also
on the cross member frame. The cross-member will have spiked teeth to
pierce the sheet.
• MTC with chiller has to be connected to the copper pipes
• There will also be small air jet nozzles in the upper frame for cooling the frame
after the heating
• The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final
formed part and place it over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading
automatically
5.1 Sheet Clamping Electro-Servo Motor Driven
5.2 Bottom Frame Adjustment Universal Type with Temperature Control
5.3 Top Frame Adjustment Automatic Motorised with Temperature Control & air jets
6. Mould Table, Clamping System & Plug Assist:
image v. Bottom Table – Servo Motor Driven & Top Table – Pneumatic Driven

[PAGE 6]
Image vi. connection of ancilliary equipment –
MTC with water connection
Image vii. pneumatic tool clamping system
§ There are 2 x tables present on the machine – below & above the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is usually loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
§ Clamping is to be done using pneumatic system
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
6.1 Bottom Table Movement Electric Servo Motor Driven
Electric using 2 x Servo Motors
The table can be stopped at any distance between 0 to max.
stroke – for products with shorter height
The table can have acceleration / deceleration possible for a
soft release of the tool after forming and also a soft touch
town with the heated sheet
Results in faster cycle time
Clamping Force: 30 KN
Max. Tool weight: 900 kg
6.2 Tool Clamping Using Special Pneumatic Tool Clamping system in the centre
of the table
6.3 Number of Holes for Vacuum 3 x vacuum holes – center & diagonal – 2’’ each all
connected using a manual ball valve in the front to slect
which ones to open/close

[PAGE 7]
6.4 Top Table Movement Using 2 x pneumatic cylinders – height adjustment motorized
using setting available on touchscreen HMI
6.5 Plug Asist Clamping Using bolts on the upper platen
7. Cooling System:
image xii. cooling fans
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
7.1. Cooling System Type 8 x centrifugal blowers
7.2. Sheet Tmperature Sensing (Cooling) 1 x Pyrometer to measure final temperature of the cooled
part
8. Vacuuming System
image xv. vacuum pump + tank in back of the machine with servo valve

2. Heater System:
image i. IR ceramic heater oven
§ The most important objective of a thermoforming machine is the heating of the thermoplastic sheet –
there are 2 x heater ovens – top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI

§

2.3 Heater Size 245 x 63 mm
2.4 Heater Wattage 500 W each in Top
& 350 W each in Bottom
2.5 No. of Heater Elements per Zone 2
2.6 Heater Control System SSR controlled by PLC
2.7 Heater Movement Type Pneumatic Cylinder driven
2.8 Heater Grid Layout 8 x 16
3. Preblow & Sag control:
image ii. preblow control image iii. sag control
§ The machine is designed as per European concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming
area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature
to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet and sag photocell is below the sheet
3.1 Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
3.2 Sag Control System 1 x Photocells below the sheet

image iv. MS welded Frames
§ MS Welded Fixed Frames Bottom Frame - Individual Sized as per sheet size – to be stored as per the
sheet sizes to be used in production
§ 1 x MS Welded Frame of full forming area will be provided with the machine
§ Bottom Frame is bolted with C-Clamps on the machine by the operator before the start of production
§ Top Frame will be adjusted using cross members
§ The top frame also has pneumatic cylinders that lift the final formed part using the sheet clamping and
place it over the automatic sheet loader at the end of the cycle
4.1 Sheet Loading Manually by Operator
4.2 Sheet Clamping Pneumatic Driven
4.3 Sheet Size Setting Using MS Welded Fixed Frames
5. Mould Table, Clamping System & Plug Assist:
§ The vacuum forming mould is loaded on the bottom table of the machine & the plug assist is located on
the top table – both the movements are using pneumatic cylinders
§ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
§ The plug assist is clamped using bolts on the upper table
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
PF1 Series stands for Polymer Forming machine. Machinecraft displayed the first PF1 series at the K Show
in Dusseldorf in 1998. Machinecraft has built PF1 Series in different sizes and options - has installed them
globally in India, Middle East, Europe and North America. Each machine is custom tailor made as per the
client’s request – size, movements and automation options.
The machine in the photo is just a reference photo of a similar spec “XL” sized machine. The thermoforming
machine is of max. forming area 4.25 x 2 m in this photo.
The standard machine colour is light grey RAL 7035 and black grey RAL 7021 with important machine parts
being painted in signal yellow RAL 1003
Below we have prepared a custom made machine offer as per specs provided by you
1. PF1 4000 x 2000 x 600
1.1 Max. Forming Area 4000 x 2000 mm
1.2 Max Tool Height in Bottom Table 600 mm
1.3 Max Plug Assist Height below sheet level 300 mm

2. Heater System:
image i. sandwitch heater ovens image ii. quartz heating elements

▪ The machine comes with sandwitch heaters – which means 2 x heater ovens in top & bottom of the
forming area to heat the thermoplastic sheet. So you can thermoform plastic sheet upto 10 mm
thickness without any problems.
▪ Independent moving top & bottom heater ovens
▪ The heater ovens have infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

3. Preblow & Sag control:
image iii. preblow control image iv. sag control
▪ The machine is designed as per European concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming
area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature
to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
▪ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
▪ Preblow photocell is above the sheet and sag photocell is below the sheet

Automatic Sheet Loader System
▪ The first step is to load the plastic sheet into the machine – this is done using a special design
automatic sheet loader
▪ The Autoloader robot placed on the right/left side of the machine – as per customer choice.
▪ The autoloader first centers the sheet, then makes sure that only 1 x sheet is lifted each time using a
separator mechanism
▪ The autoloader picks 1 x thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using an up/down
& in/out mechanism which are both servo motor driven
▪ After the forming cycle, the autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
▪ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up
Universal Frame System
▪ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system
▪ The operator can enter the sheet size on the HMI and the bottom and top frames – driven by 4 x servo
motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
▪ There will be 3 x sets of universal frames available with the machine to be changed using a forklift to
achieve all possible size reductions
▪ Additional reduction can be possible with additional universal frames
▪ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass
▪ They also have cooling lines with copper pipes on them
Frame No. Max. Forming (mm) Min. Forming (mm)

UF02 3000 x 2000 2000 x 1500
UF03 2000 x 1500 1000 x 1000

supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for

controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out and the working pressure is
monitored.

A. Technical Description of the system
Quote for production of housing of Porta-Toilets using the thermoforming process
The walls of the porta-toilet can be manufactured using the following process
Thermoforming HDPE Part run-off trimming on
sheet CNC router
But the door frame & the door of the toilet needs to have more strength as they bear a lot of more force
than the walls, so they have to be 2-sheet thermoformed using the following process
Part run-off Ultra Sonic
Thermoforming
trimming on Welding for 2-
HDPE sheet
CNC router sheet part
The quote below consists of details of
i. Vacuum Forming Machine
ii. CNC Trimming Router Machine
iii. US Welding System
iv. Moulds for Vacuum Forming
v. Fixture for CNC Trimming
vi. Fixture for US Welding

5. Sheet Size Setting System & Sheet Loading:
a. Sheet Size Setting System
■ MS Welded Fixed Frames Bottom Frame - Individual Sized as per sheet size – to be stored as per the
sheet sizes to be used in production – 3 x frames to suit the 3 x products will be provided with the
machine
■ MS Welded Frames for the walls, door frame, door & roof will be provided with the machine
■ Bottom Frame is bolted with C-Clamps on the machine by the operator before the start of production
■ Top Frame has adjustable cross-members to match to the bottom frame – to be adjusted manually
■ The top frame also has pneumatic cylinders that lift the final formed part using the sheet clamping and
place it over the automatic sheet loader at the end of the cycle
b. Automatic Sheet Loader
■ Sheet Centering is to be done by the operator using chrome rods adjustable using bolts at the start of
the cycle after loading the heap of thermoplastic sheets into the autoloader
■ The autoloaders job is to make sure that the machine is fed with the thermoplastic sheet and the final
part is taken out of the machine – simultaneously
■ The autoloader makes sure that only 1 x sheet is lifted each time using a separator mechanism
■ The autoloader picks 1 x thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using an up/down
& in/out mechanism which are both servo motor driven
■ After the forming cycle, the autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
■ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up
www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 17

§ Upper Table movement using Pneumatic Cylinder – to press against the preblow box to allow pre-
blowing bubble
§ There will be 2 x heater banks in top & bottom – sandwitch heating
§ Heater oven is static – the sheet goes inside the oven to be heated
§ Sag control when sheet is being heated is not present – hence thermoplastic like ABS can be
thermoformed on this type of machine

§ Heating element type: IR Ceramic Heating Elements, Make: European
§ Heater control is using SSR Control System – SSR Make: Unison
Process Flow
1. Operator will place the sheet on the machine. The clamping area of the sheet will be in the
thermoforming machine. After that 2 switches have to be pressed by the operator for starting
the forming cycle.
2. Sheet is clamped tight by pneumatic cylinders
3. As soon as the sheet is clamped the heater – it goes inside the heater oven – in the middle of
the machine and the sheet is heated for Set Time and Temperature.
4. Sheet can sag freely when heated and is not controlled during the heating cycle
5. Once the sheet is heated to the set tempearture, the sheet clamp frames comes back in front
area – the upper table comes down to press against the preblow box – to allow pre-blowing of
the heated sheet
6. After some time the sheet is cooled using centrifugal blowers

text:
[PAGE 1]
Dear Sir
Thank you very much for your inquiry. Today we have the pleasure to transmit our quote.
Quote No. 2023003
Date 02/03/2023
Contact partner Rushabh Doshi
Mobile +91 9833112903
Email rushabh@machinecraft.org
Kindly consider our explanations, we are convinced that our concept meets your requirements.
Best regards
RDDoshi
i.V.
Technical Sales Manager

[PAGE 2]
1. Quote Basis
1.1 Your scope of work
Your inquiry FCS machine to make blisters & clamshell with 0.15 mm PVC & PET sheets
Customer specifications FCS 500 x 650 mm
1.2 Machine layout
FCS stands for Form-Cut-Stack machine type – multistation thermoforming machine – there are 4 stations
– stations being heating, forming, holing, punching and stacking. The machine is fully servo driven and runs
at dry cycle speed of upto 30 cycles per minute.
FCS Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The FCS Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 11

[PAGE 3]
2. Machinery Description
■ FCS Machine – with forming area 500 x 650 mm and max. product depth 140 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine. This loop is sensed by a cat-whiskers type limit switch
to stop the motor. The mechanical limit switch is offered as it is able to sense transparent plastic
materials too. The unwinder can hold sheet roll of max. width 650 mm and weight 400 kgs.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox. The film from the in-feed is transported into different
stations by the spike chain accurately using a servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/-
0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Dual/Sandwich Heating System with Top and Bottom heaters. Heaters can be moved
into the forming station so that they are as near the mould as possible. This is important for
uniformity in heating of the sheet to be formed.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements from Elstein Germany
■ Heater Elements are placed in 6 rows and 8 columns i.e 48 heating elements in top & bottom each
■ Top Heater: 500 W each, Bottom heater: 350 W each
■ Total Heating Load: 40.8 KW
■ Forming Station (Station 1)
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using servo motors
■ There will be a helping frame included in the forming station
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
■ The forming press has a pressure box in top/bottom connected to a tank that generates pressure
upto 4 bar. The pressure can be adjusted using pressure regulator
■ The air release is regulated using a pneumatic cylinder controlled as per setting done on the HMI
■ Holing Station (Station 2)
■ The hole cutting die is mounted on the holing platen
■ The holing platens are driven up/down by the holing press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ Punching Station (Station 3)
■ The steel ruled cutting die is mounted on the punching platen
■ The punching platens are driven up/down by the punching press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ The punching force of upto 60 tonnes is generated by the toggle design
■ There is a chain mechanism for height adjustment in the punching press
■ For fine aligning of the punching tool, there is a special xy mechanism on linear guide
■ The XY setting is motorized using hand knobs
■ Temperature controller and provision will be there in puching station so that you can have heated
punching tool ideal for PET cutting
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 11

[PAGE 6]
■ The holing and punching station distances are adjustable and driven using servo motors – hence this
too can be stored in the form of receipe as per the product
■ The lubrication in the presses is automatic
■ Stacking Station (Station 4)
■ Up Stacking using Pneumatic Motion
■ Operator can set a fixed number of items and the pusher will then put them up before stacking more
below – double layer
■ There will be pneumatic push out with coveyor present in the machine
■ Trim Winding
■ The trim-winding Station winds the left-over plastic sheet trim after punching
■ The winder is driven by a geared motor of 1/2 HP
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 10" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI box is on a swivel mechanism
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 11

[PAGE 7]
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 11

[PAGE 8]

a. FCS500/650 multi-station fully automatic / servo thermoforming machine
125,00,000 INR, ex-works India

2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance.
■ Basic Technical Specs

■ Chain Indexing
■ The cut-sheet is to be loaded in the in-feed section
■ The in-feed area will have sheet edge heaters fitted, each a seprate zone
■ The material is indexed in spiked chain of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox
■ The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the
machine
■ The chain rails are water cooled
■ Heating Oven
■ The sheet first enters the heating station
■ Machine has Single Heater Oven in Top & Bottom Both – moved using high temperature pneumatic
cylinder
■ Heaters can be setup in Zones using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be
modified individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements are European make

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Heater element size is 265 x 95 mm
■ Heater wattage is 500 W each (Top), 350 W (Bottom)
■ There will be 5-pitch heating
■ There are 9 x 8 = 72 heater elements in the heating oven
■ The heaters are controlled using SSR which are controlled using the PLC system & PID logic
■ There is a sag sensor at the bottom of the sheet to detect excessive sagging and the chain indexing
stops and a notification alarm pops up on the HMI for the operator to stop the machine and restart
■ There will be a pyrometer in the heating oven which will indicate the true temperature of the sheet
on the HMI
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped using a spring loaded clamping system
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using servo motors
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 9

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ The forming press is capable of pressure forming, so the platen is connected to a pressure box
capable of applying a max. pressure of upto 6.8 bar
■ The forming press is rated to max. 18 tonnes at 6.8 bar
■ Top platen: 350mm stroke
■ Bottom platen: 150mm stroke
■ Top platen with extra movement at top for stripper plate – with max. coining pressure 22 tonnes
■ There will be a 3rd motion plug drive using servo motor for extra movement below the bottom plate
■ Pneumatics
■ The machine will have servo valve to control the pressure form
■ There will also be a separate vacuum valve to pull main vacuum through the cavities
■ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is
to supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air
supply for the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is
reduced in a controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out
■ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
■ Cooling System
■ There will be supply manifolds mounted at top & bottom tols for water cooling
■ Quick disconnect fittings will be sued with manual shut off valves for each circuit, supply & return
■ Mainfolds have gauges showing temperature , pressure & flow rates on each manifold, 4 sets in
total
■ Tool Temperature sensing
■ The machine will have abiolity to record temperature of tool for coining, tool cooling zones, chain rail
cooling using thermocouple s
■ The machine will have 30 zones minimum
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC Control
■ 10" HMI color touchscreen - Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI

Process 1: IMG-L Thermoforming for 2/2 cavity tool layout
Machinecraft’s PF1X Series is compact yet powerful thermoforming machine capable of IMG-L progams –
fitted with IR quartz heater elements, machine movements – electric servo motor driven, vacuum pump +
tank & waterlines for both tables, optional trimming table upper for trimming – all process parameters
settable on touch-screen HMI and machine controlled using PLC system.
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specs for PF1X-1006
1.1 Max. Sheet Size 1000 x 600 mm
1.2 Max Tool Height / Daylight Opening 1100 mm
1.3 Total Max. Heater Load 37.5 KW
1.4 Servo Motor Load 30 KW
1.5 Total Max. Connected Load (approx.) 70 KW
1.6 Vacuum Pump Capacity 2 x 40 m3/hr
2. Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The first step is to load the IMG-L tool on the machine
§ The operator presses a button on the HMI to enter the tool load mode
§ The machine has daylight to move the complete IMG-L tool inside the machine

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The bottom table will have Ball Transfer units with pneumatic cylinders that are first lifted to be able to
slide the IMG tool on the machine
§ Once the tool is loaded, the bottom plug assist tool is clamped using pneumatic cylinders in the centre
of the table – by the operator – there is a poka-yoke system with which once the fixture is loaded, the
machine switches off and no movements can happen while the operator is clamping the bottom tool.
§ Once he clamps the bottom tool, the operator presses a button to indicate that the bottom tool is
clamped
§ The bottom table then moves up and the upper table moves down, the IMG-L tool is then to be clamped
pneumatically onto the upper table from the centre - once he clamps the IMG-L tool, the operator
presses a button to indicate that the IMG-L tool is clamped
§ Total tool change-over time is less than 20 minutes
3. Automatic Sheet Loading System for Cut-Sheets:
§ The second step is to load the foil on the forming area
§ The foil is kept on a stack on the trolley of the autoloader – precut foils are kept as sheets in the
autoloader
§ Frame with Vacuum pads comes down using 1 x servo motor to pick up 1 x foil and goes up to place it
on the chain
§ The sheet is clamped onto the chain system which transports the foil into the forming area

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The spike-chain is driven using 1 x servo motor
§ Once the part is formed, the spike chain transports the skin into an unloading area on the side of the
machine
4. Sheet Clamping
§ Once the sheet is loaded, it is then clamped tightly using 2 x pneumatic cylinders and a clamp frame
§ The clamp frame is steel welded – of the size of the sheet with rubber groove to add rubber for sealing
§ There are 2 x frames for sheet size setting on the machine – the bottom frame is aluminum CNC
machined frame and the Top Frame comes with adjustable members
5. Heater System:
§ The most important objective of a thermoforming machine is the heating of the thermoplastic sheet
homogeneously – this is possible with zone heating
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets from
both sides – the upper side of the foil is the A-side & lower side is the B-side
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heater movement is servo motor driven (top & bottom) – the heaters come in front ontop of the
forming area to heat the sheet
§ In-case of power failure there is an air-safety tank that uses the pneumatic cylinders to takes the heater
to the home position (for safety)

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
6.1 Heater Type Infrared IR Quartz
6.2 Heater Size 245 x 63 mm
6.3 Heater Wattage 500 W each (Top)
750 W each (Bottom)
6.4 No. of Heater Elements per Zone 1 (Individual Heater Control)
6.5 Heater Control System SSR with PID logic
6.6 Heater Movement Type Using 1 x Servo Motor of 1 KW for
Top heating oven & 1 x Servo Motor
of 1 KW for Bottom heating oven
6.7 Sheet Temperature Sensing 1 x Pyrometer placed in centre of the
heating oven in top & bottom each
6. Moving Tables on the Machine
There are 2 x tables on the machine – lower, upper
§ Table 1 – Lower Table – clamping force of 30 KN – 2 x 5.5 KW Servo Motors
§ Table 2 – Upper Table – clamping force of 10 KN – 1 x 5.5 KW Servo Motor
§ Table 1 – Lower Table – for substrate holding with vacuum
§ Table 2 – Upper Table 1 – for IMG-L forming
§ All tables are driven using electric servo motor & tables have guide rods – for perfect alignment of the
tables – required for IMG-L forming cycle
§ Both the tables have provisions of vacuum & water lines to connect MTC + Chiller to the tool using
connectors as per tool specification

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
7. Cooling System:
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
8.1 Cooling System Type 4 x centrifugal fans
8.2 Sheet Temperature Sensing (Cooling) 1 x Pyrometer to measure final temperature of the cooled
part
8. Vacuuming System
§ There are 2 x vacuum pumps with 2 x tanks – one for the lower table and one for IMG forming in upper
table
§ The machine comes with a genereosuly dimensioned vacuum tank
§ The tank is connected to vacuum pump
§ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is to
supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air supply for
the pneumatic system.
§ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding
§ There is a servo valve for smooth vacuuming with digital reading on the HMI
9.1 Vacuum Pump Capacity 2 x 40 cubic meter per hour
9.2 Vacuum Pump Type Oil-Lubricated Rotary Vane Vacuum Pumps

[PAGE 8]

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

§ Heating controlled as individual or total field adjustment

§ Door lock safety swtich

Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)
b. Tool Loading & Clamping

support channels

cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish

mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

touchscreen HMI

Machine to produce 3D car mats with TPE material - 2 mm max. thickness roll on a continuous type
vacuum forming machine
1.2 Machine layout
AMC Series is a large format roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the
sheet from heating to forming and to the shot cutting station
Sheet Sheet Vacuum Shot
Heating Cooling
unwinding Clamping Forming Cutting
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 9

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance.
■ Basic Technical Specs
■ max. forming 2000 x 1600 mm
■ max. product depth 150 mm
■ Roll Unwinder
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed.
■ Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material pulled forms a loop in front of the
main machine.
■ Chain Indexing
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section
■ The material is indexed in spiked chain of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox
■ The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the
machine
■ Heating Ovens
■ The sheet first enters the heating station
■ Machine has Single Heater Oven in Top – moved using high temperature pneumatic cylinder
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 9

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Heaters can be setup in Zones using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be
modified individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements are European make
■ Heater element size is 265 x 95 mm
■ Heater wattage is 500 W each
■ There are 8 x 16 = 128 heater elements in the heating oven
■ The heaters are controlled using SSR which are controlled using the PLC system & PID logic
■ There is a sag sensor at the bottom of the sheet to detect excessive sagging and the chain indexing
stops and a notification alarm pops up on the HMI for the operator to stop the machine and restart
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped using a spring loaded clamping system
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 9

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
■ Cooling System
■ There are 2 x centrifugal fans with water mist present in the forming station for fast cooling after the
forming
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter – cuts each shot from the machine to be then individually cut using a steel
rule die on a hydraulic press
■ Vacuum System
■ 100 m3/hr vacuum pump with tank
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC Control
■ 10" HMI color touchscreen
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI

[PAGE 1]
CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
THE UNDERSIGNED:
1. Jupiduu Gmbh a company organized and existing under the laws of Germany, with its
registered office and its official address at Am Freibad 1 | 46499 Hamminkeln, hereby
duly represented by Ken Buschmann (the Disclosing Party); and
2. Formpack a company organized and existing under the laws of INDIA with its
registered office and its official address at 505 PALM SPRINGS MALAD W MUMBAI
400064 INDIA hereby duly represented by RUSHABH DOSHI);
Each hereinafter individually also referred to as a Party and together as the Parties.
WHEREAS:
(A) Jupiduu Gmbh is a company that develops high quality children’s toys that have
been making children’s eyes light up since 2014
(B) FORMPACK is a company that MANUFACTURES Thermoformed parts using plastic
material as a sub-contractor
(C) In order to enable the Parties to jointly explore a possible future cooperation between
them on the field of part development using thermoforming (the Purpose) it is
anticipated that a Party by itself and/or through its Affiliates (as defined in Section 4
hereinafter) (the Disclosing Party) may disclose to the other Party (the Receiving
Party) Confidential Information (as defined in Section 1 hereinafter) and the
Disclosing Party wishes to ensure that such Confidential Information is subject to the
provisions of this confidentiality agreement (the Agreement).
HEREBY AGREES AS FOLLOWS:
1. Information such as CAD data disclosed to the other Party, before or after the date of
entering into this Agreement, whether in writing or in the form of drawings, orally or
electronically or in any other way, is hereinafter referred to as Confidential
Information.
2. The Receiving Party:
i) acknowledges that the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information is confidential
and proprietary to the Disclosing Party;
ii) undertakes to maintain the confidentiality of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential
Information;
iii) shall protect the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information and any copies made
thereof against disclosure, theft and unauthorized access in the same manner and
with the same degree of care, but not less than a reasonable degree of care, with
which it protects Confidential Information of its own;

[PAGE 2]
iv) shall use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information solely for the Purpose and
for the conduct of consultations and negotiations in relation thereto;
v) shall not exploit or use the Confidential Information, in whole or in part, whether in
modified form or not, for the purpose of developing (derivative) products/services
for itself or third parties;
vi) shall not alter, decompile, disassemble, attempt to decipher or otherwise reverse
engineer any Confidential Information or any part thereof, nor permit others to do
so; and
vii) shall not use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in any way
detrimental to the Disclosing Party’s interests.
3. The Receiving Party may disclose the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information
without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent only to the extent that such
information:
i) is already known to the Receiving Party before the date of disclosure under this
Confidentiality Agreement and not subject to any confidentiality obligation
towards the Disclosing Party;
ii) is already in possession of the public or becomes available to the public other than
through the Receiving Party’s breach of this Confidentiality Agreement;
iii) is required to be disclosed under any applicable law, court order or direction, or
governmental or regulatory authority regulation, rule or requirement;
iv) is required to be disclosed by the rules and regulations of any recognized stock
exchange upon which the Receiving Party's or its Affiliates' stock is quoted; or
v) is acquired lawfully from a third party which is not subject to any confidentiality
obligation towards the Disclosing Party;
vi) is developed independently by the Receiving Party without having any knowledge
of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.
4. This Agreement shall be effective from 1st April 2023 for a period of [3 (three)] years.
5. Tool Ownership: The ownership of the tool belongs to Jupiduu Gmbh and will only be
used to produce parts for Jupiduu Gmbh as per their orders. It will be stored with
utmost security at Formpack’s tool storage unit. Once the order is complete and if
Jupiduu demands for the tool to be sent back to them, then Formpack will do so by
properly packing it
6. Amendments of, and addenda to, this Confidentiality Agreement are only valid if in
writing and signed by the duly authorized representatives of both Parties.
7. This Agreement comprises the full and complete agreement of the Parties with
respect to the subject matter hereof and cancels all prior communications,
understandings and agreements between the Parties relating thereto, whether written
or oral, expressed or implied.

[PAGE 3]
8. If any provision or part of a provision of this Confidentiality Agreement is found by a
court or authority of competent jurisdiction to be void or unenforceable, that provision
or part of a provision shall be deemed to be deleted from this Confidentiality
Agreement and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
AS AGREED UPON IN TWOFOLD AND SIGNED BY:
Jupiduu Gmbh FORMPACK

4. Automatic Sheet Loading
image iii. Automatic sheet loader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system to make sure only 1 x sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table
§ There will be sheet centering system in the automatic sheet loader
