Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design,
appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region
• Machines are capable of being connected to the internet for remote monitoring in-case of any
failure in the machine operation and to get it up and running again within 24 hours

4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
Quote no.: 2022121006 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 16

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
12 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety

2. Your benefits
Based on your requirements we have prepared an optimum concept. Our technology quotes the following
benefits:
PF1 Series Single Station machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more than 25 years
of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The PF1 Series - plastic sheet thermoformers offer a concept
that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency, operating
convenience and process reliability.
■ PF1 Machine – with forming area 4500 x 2500 mm, depth 500 mm
■ Tool Change System – tool must be changed using a forklift
■ Tool Clamping will happen using pneumatic cylinders in the bottom of tool table – this is important to be
pneumatic as it allows a soft locking of the aluminum tool – making this feature servo is not possible
and not recommended as it may break the mould if made servo driven
■ Sheet Loading – Manual with Operator and Light Guard Safety
■ Bottom table electric servo motor driven – the table can be stopped at any distance between the sheet
and lowest point – operator can enter parameters like acceleration and deceleration in the auto cycle
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 13

[PAGE 4]
■ Top table is servo driven – this is for Plug assist feature – useful for negative thermoforming process
■ Sheet clamping frame is is servo driven – to tightly clamp the sheet onto the sheet frame
■ Bottom Frame can be adjusted automatically with setting on the touchscreen – there will be 8 x pusher
boxes in the bottom of the sheet loading area – the boxes are driven by servo motors which adjust
aluminium plates on top to the size of sheet set by the operator
■ Max. Sheet Size: 4500 x 2500 mm
■ Min. Sheet Size: 2000 x 1500 mm
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 13

[PAGE 5]
■ To process sheets of sizes smaller than 2000 x 1500, Individual Mild Steel Welded Frame has to be
loaded on top of the adjustable bottom frames
■ Top Frame for sheet clamping is adjusted by servo motors and setting done on the HMI screen
automatically – top frame can be separately operated from the bottom frame
■ Max. Size: 4500 mm x 2500 mm
■ Min. Size: 500 x 1000 mm
■ Easily operated HMI control system with 10’ touchscreen as operator interface
■ Visualisation of heater bank settings and all other process parameters
■ Heating controlled as individual or total field adjustment
■ Heater banks are automatically set as per the sheet size set for the universal frame settings
■ There will be 2 x heater banks – top and bottom – to heat the thermoplastic sheet from both sides

heating to the thermoplastic sheet

THERMOFORMING MACHINE PF1X SERIES
Machinecraft’s PF1X Series is compact yet powerful thermoforming machine capable of IMG-L progams –
fitted with IR quartz heater elements, machine movements – electric servo motor driven, vacuum pump +
tank & waterlines for both tables, optional trimming table upper for trimming – all process parameters
settable on touch-screen HMI and machine controlled using PLC system.
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specs for PF1X-1006
1.1 Max. Sheet Size 1000 x 600 mm
1.2 Max Tool Height / Daylight Opening 1100 mm
1.3 Total Max. Heater Load 20 KW
1.4 Servo Motor Load 30 KW
1.5 Total Max. Connected Load (approx.) 55 KW
1.6 Vacuum Pump Capacity 2 x 40 m3/hr
2. Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The first step is to load the IMG tool on the machine
§ The operator presses a button on the HMI to enter the tool load mode
§ The machine has daylight to move the complete IMG-S tool inside the machine
Quote no.: 2022081901 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 10

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The bottom table will have Ball Transfer units with pneumatic cylinders that are first lifted to be able to
slide the IMG tool on the machine
§ Once the tool is loaded, the bottom plug assist tool is clamped using pneumatic cylinders in the centre
of the table – by the operator – there is a poka-yoke system with which once the fixture is loaded, the
machine switches off and no movements can happen while the operator is clamping the bottom tool.
§ Once he clamps the bottom tool, the operator presses a button to indicate that the bottom tool is
clamped
§ The bottom table then moves up and the upper table moves down, the IMG-S tool is then to be
clamped pneumatically onto the upper table from the centre - once he clamps the IMG-S tool, the
operator presses a button to indicate that the IMG-S tool is clamped
§ Total tool change-over time is less than 30 minutes
3. Sheet Loading:
§ The second step is to load the foil on the forming area
§ The foil is kept on a stack on the table of the autoloader using the forklift – precut foils are kept as
sheets in the autoloader
§ Frame with Vacuum pads comes down using 1 x servo motor to pick up 1 x foil and goes up to place it
on the gripper frame, there is a support frame below the gripper frame so that the foil does not fall down
§ The Clamp Frame then goes inside the forming area using 1 x servo motor in/out movement
4. Special Stetch Grippers
§ The machine will have special electro-pneumatic grippers that are holding the foil
§ Once the foil is heated, the grippers can slide out to stretch the material by upto 30 mm on each side
§ This results in overall foil saving of upto 60 mm in length & 60 mm in width
Quote no.: 2022081901 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 10

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
5. Sheet Clamping
§ Once the sheet is loaded, the opearator presses a buttom on the HMI screen
§ The foil is then clamped tightly using 2 x pneumatic cylinders and a clamp frame
§ The clamp frame is steel welded – of the size of the sheet with rubber groove to add rubber for sealing
image iii. Sheet Frame on the Machine
§ There are 2 x frames for sheet size setting on the machine – the bottom frame is aluminum CNC
machined frame and the Top Frame comes with adjustable members
§ The operator has to change the frame as per the sheet size – there will be groove for the rubber in
the frame
§ 1 x set of bottom aluminum frame - will be provided with the machine
6. Heater System:
image i. IR quartz heaters – Zone Controlled
using HMI touchscreen – IR Probe
§ The most important objective of a thermoforming machine is the heating of the thermoplastic sheet
homogeneously – this is possible with zone heating
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets from
both sides – the upper side of the foil is the A-side & lower side is the B-side
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heater movement is servo motor driven (top & bottom)

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader

a. Static Type Sheet Size Setting Frames

■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
■ The tool is expected to be loaded using a fork-lift and place the tool on top of the bottom table using
support channels
■ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
■ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – Manual
■ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet into the machine manually and then starts the auto-
cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish
■ Once the part is formed, he takes it out of the machine
b. Heating Ovens
■ The machine comes with double heater ovens in top and bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto 10
mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
■ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI

§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” using pulsated air is possible in the machine
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated
sheet – to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet

Machinecraft will be desiging a special type PF1 Series for ANP – to be able to successfully vacuum form
high-sag HDPE sheet to make bedliner. Machine will be open type – without chamber – but will have the
bottom heater move up/down as per the sheet sag distance to optimally heat the HDPE sheet.

forming area to heat the thermoplastic sheet

to allow sagging of HDPE sheet

heater will move down using a servo motor mechanism
§ The heater ovens have infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

Mechanism

a. Sheet Size Setting System
§ MS Welded Fixed Frames Bottom Frame - Individual Sized as per sheet size – to be stored as per the
sheet sizes to be used in production

§ Top Frame is adjustable manually using Cross-Members
§ The top frame also has pneumatic cylinders that lift the final formed part using the sheet clamping and
place it over the automatic sheet loader at the end of the cycle
b. Automatic Sheet Loader
§ The autoloaders job is to make sure that the machine is fed with the thermoplastic sheet and the final
part is taken out of the machine – simultaneously
§ The autoloader picks 1 x thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using an up/down
& in/out mechanism which are both servo motor driven as shown in images above
§ Sheet Centering is to be done by the operator using chrome rods adjustable using bolts at the start of
the cycle after loading the heap of thermoplastic sheets into the autoloader
Quote no.: MT2022032102 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 11

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The autoloader makes sure that only 1 x sheet is lifted each time using a separator mechanism
§ After the forming cycle, the autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up
c. Sheet Clamping
§ Once the sheet is loaded, it is clamped tightly onto the frame
§ The clamp frame that tightly clamps the thermoplastic sheet is servo motor driven

and can also be stopped at any height between the

1. Quote Basis
1.1 Your scope of work
Your inquiry FCS machine to make blisters & clamshell with 0.15 mm PVC & PET sheets
Customer specifications FCS 500 x 650 mm
1.2 Machine layout
FCS stands for Form-Cut-Stack machine type – multistation thermoforming machine – there are 4 stations
– stations being heating, forming, holing, punching and stacking. The machine is fully servo driven and runs
at dry cycle speed of upto 30 cycles per minute.
FCS Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The FCS Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 11

[PAGE 3]
2. Machinery Description
■ FCS Machine – with forming area 500 x 650 mm and max. product depth 140 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine. This loop is sensed by a cat-whiskers type limit switch
to stop the motor. The mechanical limit switch is offered as it is able to sense transparent plastic
materials too. The unwinder can hold sheet roll of max. width 650 mm and weight 400 kgs.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1"
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox. The film from the in-feed is transported into different
stations by the spike chain accurately using a servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/-
0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Dual/Sandwich Heating System with Top and Bottom heaters. Heaters can be moved
into the forming station so that they are as near the mould as possible. This is important for
uniformity in heating of the sheet to be formed.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ IR Heating Elements from Elstein Germany
■ Heater Elements are placed in 6 rows and 8 columns i.e 48 heating elements in top & bottom each
■ Top Heater: 500 W each, Bottom heater: 350 W each
■ Total Heating Load: 40.8 KW
■ Forming Station (Station 1)
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using servo motors
■ There will be a helping frame included in the forming station
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 11

[PAGE 5]
■ The forming press has a pressure box in top/bottom connected to a tank that generates pressure
upto 4 bar. The pressure can be adjusted using pressure regulator
■ The air release is regulated using a pneumatic cylinder controlled as per setting done on the HMI
■ Holing Station (Station 2)
■ The hole cutting die is mounted on the holing platen
■ The holing platens are driven up/down by the holing press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ Punching Station (Station 3)
■ The steel ruled cutting die is mounted on the punching platen
■ The punching platens are driven up/down by the punching press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo motors
■ The punching force of upto 60 tonnes is generated by the toggle design
■ There is a chain mechanism for height adjustment in the punching press
■ For fine aligning of the punching tool, there is a special xy mechanism on linear guide
■ The XY setting is motorized using hand knobs
■ Temperature controller and provision will be there in puching station so that you can have heated
punching tool ideal for PET cutting
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 11

[PAGE 6]
■ The holing and punching station distances are adjustable and driven using servo motors – hence this
too can be stored in the form of receipe as per the product
■ The lubrication in the presses is automatic
■ Stacking Station (Station 4)
■ Up Stacking using Pneumatic Motion
■ Operator can set a fixed number of items and the pusher will then put them up before stacking more
below – double layer
■ There will be pneumatic push out with coveyor present in the machine
■ Trim Winding

3. Machine-Startup

the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area

requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames

b. Tool Loading & Clamping

of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The PF1 Series - plastic sheet thermoformers offer a concept
that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency, operating

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
• Machines are capable of being connected to the internet for remote monitoring in-case of any failure in the machine
operation and to get it up and running again within 24 hours
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specifications
Max. Sheet Dimensions mm 1550 x 2300
Max. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 1500 x 2250

2. Machine Desciption – Process Wise
I. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system – without any manual work
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
Quote no.: MT2023096 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 16

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ There are CNC machined pusher boxes with Hiwin Rail Guides that move front & back by the
connected servo motor that move the universal frames
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature control – max. temp
140 degree celcius to handle PC material
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on 2 x sides – left & right & 25 mm
thick Steel Flat part in front & back
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: max. 25 mm (left & right) and max. 40 mm (front & back)
Max. Forming Area mm 1500 x 2250
Min. Forming Area mm 750 x 750
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§
Quote no.: MT2023096 www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 16

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ There will be 4 x rows of ball transfer units – paired at 2 x distances – to handle small & large tools
§ Distance between the ball transfer unit row will be made to match the smallest size of tool to be loaded
on the machine – 750 mm
§ There will also be 2nd row of ball transfer units at a distance of 1500 mm
§ The bottom table will have aluminum plate – CNC machined with groove lines to add rubbber and also
cut-outs for ball transfer units – below which there are pneumatic cylinders that lift the transfer units to
help slide the tool
§ The mould is expected to be on a CNC machined base
§ The mould will be clamped on the bottom table using pneumatic system – 2 x hollow cylinders that lift
the tool and 1 x locking cylinder that locks the tool
§ With the pneumatic clamp system – the tool is always aligned in the centre
§ An operator has to use hand-valve to twist to unlock / lock a tool using the special pneumatic system
II. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ There will be a sheet centering system – using 2 x servo motors & lead screw system – to centre
the top sheets – in each cycle – while the sheet is being heated for the forming cycle
Quote no.: MT2023096 www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 16

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads – using an In/Out telescopic frame
§ There will be an ionizer present between the In/Out area of machine & autoloader
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
12 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
Quote no.: MT2023096 www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 16

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
§ Both heaters (upper/lower) move along independent linear guides
§ Lower Heater is protected by special ceramic glass
§ The sheet size can be seen as a visual on the heater setup screen
§ The bottom heater can be moved forward to clean it by using a small valve
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Quartz Type
Heater Size mm 250 x 65
Heater Wattage – Top W 500 (each)
Heater Wattage - Bottom W 300 (each)

construction
c. Sheet Clamping

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The sheet is clamped tightly on all 4 x sides using strong rack & pinion mechanism
§ There are 4 x racks moving in sync. via 2 x servo motors & gearboxes and vertical travel using
guides from Hiwin
§ There is a pneumatic lock to lock the clamp frame when on the upper position – for safety
§ The motors used are with electrical brakes
d. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell – in the upper area - manually height
adjustable for bubble height detection & Timer Control
for blowing air to make the bubble – available on the
touchscreen HMI
ii Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center – approx.. 150 mm below the
sheet level & 50 mm above the bottom heater
e. Table Movements:

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA

FCS Series is a roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the sheet from
heating to forming to punching & onto a conveyor.
Pushing on
Roll-feeding Heating Thermoforming Punching Conveyor Cooling
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 9

MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance.
■ Basic Technical Specs
■ max. forming area: 760 x 520 mm
■ max. product depth: 100 mm
■ Chain Indexing
■ RM is in roll, it can be placed on the roll un-winder and then loaded onto the chain
■ The material is indexed in spiked chain of 1/2" duplex type with spike repeating every 1", the spike
will piere thin sheet & for thick sheet spike will touch the surface to index the sheet
■ The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox
■ The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the
machine
■ The chain rails are water cooled
■ Heating Oven
■ The sheet first enters the heating station which is roll out design
■ The oven will fit between platens for short tool lengths
■ Machine has Heater Oven in Top & Bottom – moved using high temperature pneumatic cylinder
■ Heaters can be setup in Zones using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be
modified individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ There is light sensor to detect over sagging of sheet – and taking heater back incase of over
sagging for safety
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 9

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ IR Heating Elements are European make
■ Heater element size is 265 x 95 mm
■ Heater wattage is 500 W each
■ The heaters are controlled using SSR which are controlled using the PLC system & PID logic
■ There is a sag sensor at the bottom of the sheet to detect excessive sagging and the chain indexing
stops and a notification alarm pops up on the HMI for the operator to stop the machine and restart
■ There will be a pyrometer in the heating oven which will indicate the true temperature of the sheet
on the HMI and prevents servo plug running when sheet temperature is below a defined set point
■ Sheet Clamping
■ After the sheet is heated, the chain indexes the sheet into a sheet clamping area
■ The sheet is clamped from top & bottom
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic system
■ Punching Station
■ The steel ruled cutting die is mounted on the punching platen
■ The punching platens are driven up/down by the punching press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using electric servo-pneumatic system
■ There is a chain mechanism for alignment of the lower & upper platen in the punching press
■ For fine aligning of the punching tool, there is a special xy mechanism on linear guide
■ The XY setting is motorized using electric motors
■ Once the parts are punched they are pushed down onto a conveyor belt drive by a pneumatic
pusher
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 9

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES | 505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
■ Trim Winder
■ There will also be a trim winder to collect the run-off trimmed raw material
■ Pneumatics
■ The machine also consists of FRL Pneumatic System. The purpose of the pneumatic equipment is
to supply the system with compressed air. The maintenance unit prepares the compressed air
supply for the pneumatic system. For this purpose, the pressure in the feed line (max. 10 bar) is
reduced in a controlled manner (operating pressure 6 bar), water is separated out
■ The machine has automatic air-ejection system for part demoulding regulated pressure
■ Cooling System
■ There will be supply manifolds mounted at top & bottom tools for water cooling
■ Tempearture control using SSR & PID control giving tolerance of +/- 2 degree celcius
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC Control
■ 10" HMI color touchscreen - Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI
■ Modem for remote access/ software upgrades/troubleshooting by Machinecraft technicians

forming station of the machine

sheet is loaded

place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine – so the part is also automatically unloaded from the machine

appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements

frames

driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

You have requested a vacuum forming machine capable of forming bath products of max.

1.2 Our proposed solution
Machinecraft's PF1 Series has been In the market since 1990 and has been continually updated
with new features demanded by the customers - the latest feature being universal frames for
sheet size setting and automatic sheet loader system - which were Introduced at the K2019
Show In Germany In October 2019. Machinecraft has established a technical collaboration
with company FRIMO from Germany In 2019 to further develop the PF1 Series. The PF1 Series
Is a closed chamber type single station thermoforming machine with sandwitch heaters that
heat the thermoplastic sheet as per the profile set on the touchscreen HMI followed by "pre-
blowing" and vacuum process using the tool on the bottom and/or top tables. The machine Is
capable of processing all thermoplastic materials like ABS, PP, PC, TPO, HDPE, HIPS, etc.

important feature to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part

sheet at an even height

a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet

forming station of the machine

go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine

[PAGE 2]
1. Quote Basis
1.1 Machine layout
AM Series is a large format roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the
sheet from heating to forming and to the shot cutting station.
AM Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The AM Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of
8

[PAGE 3]
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits.
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1". The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox.
The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of
8

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Single Heating System with Top heater.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ Heater is single pitch and has max. wattage of 60 KW approx..
■ Forming Station
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
■ Max. forming area is 1250 x 1400 mm, depth of 100 mm
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter– cuts each shot from the machine
■ Vacuum System
■ Vacuum Pump & Vacuum Tank included
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 7" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI

3. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
image ii. preblow & sag control using light
sensor for even sheet thickness
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” using pulsated air is possible in the machine
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet

4. Sheet Size Setting System & Sheet Loading:
image iii. Bottom frame steel welded & Upper frame adjustable cross members
§ The sheet is loaded manually by a operator
§ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size changes,
the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on top of the main
forming area
§ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
§ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
§ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included

[PAGE 2]
1. Quote Basis
1.1 Machine layout
AM Series is a large format roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the
sheet from heating to forming and to the shot cutting station.
AM Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The AM Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 8

[PAGE 3]
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance. The machine is
fully guarded and conforms to current European safety legislation.
■ AM Series Machine – with forming area 1200 x 1200 and max. product depth 100 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1". The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox.
The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 8

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Single Heating System with Top heater.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ Forming Station (Station 1)
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter from FESTO – cuts each shot from the machine to be then individually cut
using a steel rule die on a hydraulic press
■ Vacuum System
■ Vacuum Pump & Vacuum Tank included
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 7" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI

forming area to heat the thermoplastic sheet. So you can thermoform plastic sheet upto 10 mm
thickness without any problems.
▪ Independent moving top & bottom heater ovens

homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet

Energy Saving!

to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds

to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging

motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top adjust automatically

forming station of the machine

go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism

sheet is loaded

place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads

happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine

pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine

that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency, operating
convenience and process reliability.

forming area

[PAGE 2]

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
THERMOFORMING MACHINE PF1X SERIES
Machinecraft’s PF1X Series is compact yet powerful thermoforming machine capable of IMG-S progams –
fitted with IR quartz heater elements, machine movements – electric servo motor driven, vacuum pump +
tank & waterlines for both tables, optional trimming table upper for trimming – all process parameters
settable on touch-screen HMI and machine controlled using PLC system.
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specs for PF1X-1206
1.1 Max. Sheet Size 1250 x 600 mm
1.2 Max Tool Height / Daylight Opening 1100 mm
1.3 Total Max. Heater Load 37.5 KW
1.4 Servo Motor Load 30 KW
1.5 Total Max. Connected Load (approx.) 70 KW
1.6 Vacuum Pump Capacity 2 x 40 m3/hr
2. Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The first step is to load the IMG-S tool on the machine
§ The operator presses a button on the HMI to enter the tool load mode
§ The machine has daylight to move the complete IMG-S tool inside the machine
Quote no.: 2022081901 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 9

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The bottom table will have Ball Transfer units with pneumatic cylinders that are first lifted to be able to
slide the IMG tool on the machine
§ Once the tool is loaded, the bottom plug assist tool is clamped using pneumatic cylinders in the centre
of the table – by the operator – there is a poka-yoke system with which once the fixture is loaded, the
machine switches off and no movements can happen while the operator is clamping the bottom tool.
§ Once he clamps the bottom tool, the operator presses a button to indicate that the bottom tool is
clamped
§ The bottom table then moves up and the upper table moves down, the IMG-S tool is then to be
clamped pneumatically onto the upper table from the centre - once he clamps the IMG-S tool, the
operator presses a button to indicate that the IMG-S tool is clamped
§ Total tool change-over time is less than 30 minutes
3. Automatic Sheet Loading System for Cut-Sheets:
§ The second step is to load the foil on the forming area
§ The foil is kept on a stack on the trolley of the autoloader – precut foils are kept as sheets in the
autoloader
§ Frame with Vacuum pads comes down using 1 x servo motor to pick up 1 x foil and goes up to place it
on the chain
§ The sheet is clamped onto the chain system which transports the foil into the forming area
§ The spike-chain is driven using 1 x servo motor
4. Sheet Clamping
§ Once the sheet is loaded, it is then clamped tightly using 2 x pneumatic cylinders and a clamp frame
§ The clamp frame is steel welded – of the size of the sheet with rubber groove to add rubber for sealing
Quote no.: 2022081901 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 9

I. Machine-Startup
a. Sheet Frames
■ The sheet is loaded on top of a steel welded fixed type frame – everytime the sheet size
changes, the operator must change the steel welded fixed plate and clamp it using bolts on
top of the main forming area
■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
§ The tool is to be loaded on the bottom table – and clamped using M10 bolts with the machine
II. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading and Automatic Part Unloading – using Autoloader Robot
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ The sheets have to be aligned for the first time by the operator using guide rods
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 10

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a conveyor for the operator
to pick up – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick the part off
the machine – so the part is also automatically unloaded from the machine
§ There is a pneumatic shot cutter present between the unloader and the conveyor to separate each
cavity
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heaters are servo motor driven but there will be pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of
power failure for 2-level safety

Heater Movement Type Electric Servo Motor Driven

centre of top heater oven –
isolated from the heater oven
using special construction
c. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the sheet from touhing the bottom heater and also to prevent the sheet from sagging

3. Preblow & Sag control:
image iii. preblow control image iv. sag control
▪ The machine is designed as per European concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming
area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature
to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
▪ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
▪ Preblow photocell is above the sheet and sag photocell is below the sheet

4. Sheet Loading, Clamp Frame System & final part unloading:
image v. universal bottom frame image vi. universal top frame
image vii. automatic sheet loader image viii. autoloader system
a. Automatic Sheet Loader System
▪ The first step is to load the plastic sheet into the machine – this is done using a special design
automatic sheet loader
▪ The Autoloader robot placed on the right/left side of the machine – as per customer choice.
▪ The autoloader first centers the sheet, then makes sure that only 1 x sheet is lifted each time using a
separator mechanism
▪ The autoloader picks 1 x thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using an up/down
& in/out mechanism which are both servo motor driven
▪ After the forming cycle, the autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine
▪ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine for the operator to pick up
b. Universal Frame System
▪ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system
▪ The operator can enter the sheet size on the HMI and the bottom and top frames – driven by 4 x servo
motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
▪ There will be 3 x sets of universal frames available with the machine to be changed using a forklift to
achieve all possible size reductions
▪ Additional reduction can be possible with additional universal frames
▪ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design,
appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region
• Machines are capable of being connected to the internet for remote monitoring in-case of any
failure in the machine operation and to get it up and running again within 24 hours

driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ The universal bottom frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined – driven by
pusher boxes with hiwin linear guides & servo motor driven
§ The universal top frame is motorized – lead screw driven – using servo motors

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design,
appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system directly via the HMI
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ There are CNC machined pusher boxes with Linear Rail Guides that move front & back by the
connected servo motor that move the universal frames

thick Steel Flat part in front & back

plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part

as standard

the table with the upper plates

clamped on the machine and is always in the centre

a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
Quote no.: MT2023241 www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 11

§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
§ The size of the pallet has to be smaller than the size of the sheet
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – in the front side and there will be tilted table in front to make it easy to pick
the part off the machine – so the part is also automatically unloaded from the machine
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The heaters are servo motor driven but there will be pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of
power failure for 2-level safety

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region

filename: Machinecraft_AM_1212_V01.pdf
text:
[PAGE 1]
Quote No. 2023094
Date 14/04/2023
Contact partner Rushabh Doshi
Mobile +91 9833112903
Email rushabh@machinecraft.org
Kindly consider our explanations, we are convinced that our concept meets your requirements.
Best regards
RDDoshi
i.V.
Technical Sales Manager

[PAGE 2]
1. Quote Basis
1.1 Machine layout
AM Series is a large format roll-fed automatic vacuum forming machine with chain system that drives the
sheet from heating to forming and to the shot cutting station.
AM Series – Roll Fed - thermoforming machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more
than 25 years of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The AM Series - plastic sheet thermoformers
offer a concept that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency,
operating convenience and process reliability.
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 8

[PAGE 3]
2. Basic Description
■ Machine Construction: The machine frame is constructed from substantial fully welded mild steel
sections designed to provide a solid and rigid chassis to avoid the need of special foundations or
installation pits. Front access via easy open doors for tool changing and maintenance. The machine is
fully guarded and conforms to current European safety legislation.
■ AM Series Machine – with forming area 1200 x 1200 and max. product depth 100 mm
■ Sheet Roll is placed on the unwinder system which automatically unwinds the sheet roll and forms a
loop in front of the chain in-feed. Loop Sensing System is provided on the Roll Stand. The material
pulled forms a loop in front of the main machine.
■ The Film enters the in-feed section from the unwinder section. The material is indexed in spiked chain
of 1/2" duplex type with spikes repeating every 1". The chain is driven by a servomotor and gearbox.
The film from the in-feed is transported into different stations by the spike chain accurately using a
servo motor based driven system with an accuracy of +/- 0.3 mm.
■ The chain can be adjusted as per mould size using a wrench – both at the start and end of the machine
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 8

[PAGE 4]
■ Heating Ovens
■ Machine has Single Heating System with Top heater.
■ Heater can be controlled using the touchscreen HMI. All element percentages can be modified
individually in respect to the percentage of Thermocouple elements
■ Forming Station (Station 1)
■ The forming mould is mounted on the forming platen
■ The forming platens are driven up/down by the forming press
■ The press design is toggle type and driven using pneumatic cylinders
■ The forming press is capable of vacuum forming, so the platen is connected via a tank to a vacuum
pump
■ Shot Cutter
■ A pneumatic shot cutter from FESTO – cuts each shot from the machine to be then individually cut
using a steel rule die on a hydraulic press
■ Vacuum System
■ Vacuum Pump & Vacuum Tank included
■ Main Control Panel is free standing. The control HMI panel is provided in front for easy operation.
■ PLC and Servo motors from Mitsubishi Japan
■ HMI: 7" HMI color touchscreen from Mitsubishi Japan
■ Recipes can be stored on the SD Card in the HMI
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 8

[PAGE 5]
3. Scope of supply and cost (in INR)
FCS500/650 Equipment with mentioned specs is 55,00,000 INR, ex-works India
Machine Model AM-1212
Max. Forming Area 1200 x 1200 mm
Max Forming Depth 100 mm
Sheet Thickness Range 0.3 mm to 1.4 mm
Air Pressure Using Vacuum
Forming Press Drive Pneumatic
Shot Cutter Pneumatic
Chain Indexing Servo
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 8

[PAGE 6]
4. Technical description of the scope of supply
A. Make of Components
■ PLC & HMI: Mitusbishi / Delta
■ Pneumatics: FESTO / SMC
■ Heater Elements: Stella / Ceramicx
■ Light Sensor: PnF / Sick
■ Vacuum Pump: Becker / Busch Germany
■ Servo Motors: Mitsubishi / Delta
■ Gearbox: Bonfiglioli / SEW
B. Documentation

■ Operation instructions

■ System layout
■ Maintenance instructions
■ Wiring diagram
■ Basic Spares included with the machine
All documentation are available on data medium - optionally as paper printing.
4.1 Technical supplements
4.1.1 Air supply point
Minimum of 4 bars, compressed air free of oil, water and pollution.
4.1.2 Components
Machinecraft-standard
Quote no.: D www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 8

[PAGE 7]
5. Terms of delivery and payment
5.1 Lead Time
Presently approx. 20 weeks after technical and commercial clarification.
An exact definition of the delivery times is possible only after receipt of written order confirmation and
receipt of down payments according to payment conditions.
5.2 Terms of Delivery
Transportation cost is not included in the machine price
Installation & Traning – 2 men / 5 working days – included in the price but flights, car rental,
accommodation – to be paid at actuals by the customer
5.3 Payment conditions and right to withhold payment
50% Advance along with signed PO
50% Before Dispatch
5.4 Warranty
The warranty is 12 months starting from date of pre-acceptance / notification that the scope is ready for
delivery at Machinecraft
Regular maintenance is required during the warranty period. Such work, designated as required by the
machine manufacturer, must be performed in a timely manner by the machine operator as specified in the
contract with Machinecraft. If such maintenance is not performed in a verifiable manner, the obligations of
the warranty are void.

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design, appropriate interlocks with use
of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor driven & the heaters to be
either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of components that are available in
your region
Part A: Technical Description of the Machine
1. Technical Specifications
Max. Sheet Dimensions mm 3050 x 3050
Max. Spa Size Mm 2800 x 2800
Max. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 3000 x 3000
Min. Upper Frame Dimensions mm 1500 x 1500
Max. Tool Height in Z direction mm 1000
Heater Load KW 299.2
Vacuum Pump Rate m3/hr 300
Number of Servo Motors 15
Servo Motor Load KW 49.6

Air consumption cfm 50 to 100
www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 13

[PAGE 3]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
2. Process Flow
There are 2 x parts to the process:
1. Product change-over / setup time
Frame Size Change Tool Loading & Clamping
2. Thermoforming process – in which the process of loading the sheet happens in parallel to the
thermoforming process for an optimized cycle time – possible with the use of automatic sheet
loader
Sheet Loading Sheet Clamping Heating
Vacuum Forming
Part Unloading Cooling
3. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system – without any manual work
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The bottom plates have groove with copper pipes embedded for temperature control
§ The top frame also has copper pipes embedded in front & back and also on the cross member
frame. The cross-member will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet.
§ MTC with chiller has to be connected to the copper pipes
§ There will also be small air jet nozzles in the upper frame for cooling the frame after the heating
www.machinecraft.org page 3 of 13

[PAGE 4]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on all 2 x sides
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: max. 25 mm (left & right) and max. 40 mm (front & back)
Max. Forming Area mm 3000 x 3000
Min. Forming Area mm 1500 x 1500
b. Quick Tool Loading & Clamping
§ For tool loading – the tool must be loaded on the machine using a fork-lift from the front access doors
§ The bottom table will have aluminum plate – CNC machined with groove lines to add rubbber (New!)
§ The tool is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table using pneumatic
system – 2 x hollow cylidners that lift the tool and 1 x locking cylinder that locks the tool – so the tool is
always aligned in the centre & is a quick way to clamp the tool
§ An operator has to use hand-valve to twist to unlock / lock a tool using the special pneumatic system
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – using Autoloader
§ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet on a pallet in the sheet loading area
www.machinecraft.org page 4 of 13

[PAGE 5]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ Once the sheets are aligned – the top sheet is held by vacuum suction cups and taken into the
forming station of the machine
§ The Autoloader robot placed on the right side of the machine
§ There are 2 x sensors on the autoloader – sensor 1 detects if a sheet is picked & sensor 2 detects
if multiple sheets are picked – one sensor is placed approx.. by amount equal to thickness of the
sheet below
§ The sensor has to be aligned for the first sheet – to know the sheet thickness – using adjusted
thread screw – everytime sheet thickess is changed, the sensor has to be aligned
§ The sheets are separated using a dancing frame – so 4 x cylinders are there in corners that each
go up to lift the frame one by one – so that the sheet is separated using tilting mechanism
§ There is a pneumatic sheet sepration system in the back which blows air - to make sure only 1 x
sheet is loaded
§ The autoloader picks a thermoplastic sheet and place it on top of the forming area using pick &
place mechanism – supported by vacuum pads
§ The autoloader also unloads the final formed part from the machine using the top auto frame – this
happens simultaneously along with the sheet loading into the machine
§ There is a pneumatic pusher that pushes the part out of the machine on a table for the operator to
pick up from the table – this can be in front or side
§ There will be a special Jet of Air with ionized particles for cleaning the A-surface of the sheet
between the autoloader & the main forming station
4.1 Sheet Loading Using Automatic Sheet Loader using 2 x servo motors
4.2 Sheet Cleaning Using Jet of ionized air
b. Heating Ovens
§ The machine comes with sandwitch heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto
12 mm thick
§ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
§ The heaters can be individually set or set-up in zones using setting available on the touchscreen HMI
§ The pneumatic cylinders will also be there in case of power failure for 2-level safety
§ Both heaters (upper/lower) move along independent linear guides
www.machinecraft.org page 5 of 13

[PAGE 6]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ Lower Heater is protected by special ceramic glass
§ The sheet size can be seen as a visual on the heater setup screen
Heater Type Infrared (IR) – Quartz Type
Heater Size mm 250 x 95
Heater Wattage – Top KW 187
Heater Wattage - Bottom W 112
Heater Control via SSR – solid state relay
1 x SSR connected to 2 x heater
element for Individual heater
control & Zone control using the
setting available on HMI
Heater Movement Type Servo Motor Driven but also will
have pneumatic cylinders to
retract the heaters in-case of
power failure (for safety)
Sheet Temperature Sensing Using Pyrometer
c. Preblow & Zero-Sag control:
§ The machine works on the concept of closed chamber – so the area below the forming area is
completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature to
achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds
§ “Zero Sag” feature using pulsated air is present in the machine – using pulsated air – in the chamber –
with a pneumatic cylinder system – controllable on the HMI by PLC
§ The machine also has light sensor below the plastic sheet area to control the sagging of heated sheet –
to protect the bottom heater and prevent sheet from sagging
§ Preblow photocell is above the sheet
§ Sag photocell is below the sheet
i Preblow Bubble Height Adjustment 1 x Photocell manually height adjustable & Timer
Controlled
ii Sag Control System 1 x Photocell – center
www.machinecraft.org page 6 of 13

[PAGE 7]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
d. Table Movements:
§ There are 1 x table present on the machine – below the sheet
§ The vacuum forming mould is usually loaded on the bottom table of the machine
§ The plug assist mould is usually loaded on the upper table of the machine
§ The mould table area has provision for attaching heating/cooling line from mould temperature controller
(thermoregulation device) to the vacuum forming tool
§ The thermoregulation device has to be purchased separately from third party
i Bottom Table Movement Electric Servo Motor Driven
Electric using 4 x Servo Motors
Driving mechanism – special chain type system that is easy
to maintain & has lower maintenance cost
The table can be stopped at any distance between 0 to max.
stroke – for products with shorter height
The table can have acceleration / deceleration possible for a
soft release of the tool after forming and also a soft touch
town with the heated sheet
Results in faster cycle time
Max. Tool weight: 1500 kg
www.machinecraft.org page 7 of 13

[PAGE 8]
MACHINECRAFT TECHNOLOGIES
505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
e. Cooling System:
§ After the part is vacuum formed, the machine has cooling blowers to cool the part before unloading it
i Cooling System Type Ducted & Centrally connected to a blower to pull air from
the bottom area
ii Sheet Tmperature Sensing (Cooling) 1 x Pyrometer to measure final temperature of the cooled
part
5. Vacuuming System
§ The machine comes with an integrate

image ii. preblow control image iii. sag control

area is completely sealed – air tight – to allow blowing of pulsated air – for preblow– important feature
to achieve an even wall thickness in the final part for male moulds

PF1 Series Single Station machines provide state of the art technology that is based on more than 25 years
of Machinecraft’s expertise in thermoforming. The PF1 Series - plastic sheet thermoformers offer a concept
that is superior in many technical details and convincing in terms of economical efficiency, operating
convenience and process reliability.

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region

■ The upper frame has adjustable cross-members that need to be adjusted as well
■ The fixed type of sheet size setting system typically takes 60 minutes for a product changeover and
requires the company to store all sizes of steel welded frames
■ The machine comes with 1 x full forming area steel welded frame included, more frames can be
ordered with the machine by mentioning the sheet sizes you work with (at extra cost)
b. Tool Loading & Clamping
■ The tool is expected to be loaded using a fork-lift and place the tool on top of the bottom table using
support channels
■ The mould is expected to be on a base which is to be clamped on the bottom table
■ Clamping is to be done using bolts on the 4 x sides
4. Thermoforming-Process
a. Sheet Loading – Manual
■ The operator loads the stack of plastic sheet into the machine manually and then starts the auto-
cycle – for complete automatic cycle from clamp to finish
■ Once the part is formed, he takes it out of the machine
b. Heating Ovens
■ The machine comes with double heater ovens in top & bottom – to heat thermoplastic sheets upto 10
mm thick
■ The heater oven has infrared heating elements – placed in a grid as per the forming area to allow a
homogeneous heating of the thermoplastic sheet
■ The heaters can be individually set (in pairs) or set-up in zones using setting available on the
touchscreen HMI

VACUUM FORMING MACHINE PF1 Series
Main Features of Machinecraft’s Polymer Forming (PF1) Series:
• The machine is designed to be strong with use of thick profiles, racks, shafts, gearboxes & other
mechanical components
• The machine is designed with CE-safety – upper tables are locked with 2-level safety design,
appropriate interlocks with use of sensors & special electrical safety elements
• The machine can have automation option such as automatic sheet loader & universal sheet size
frames
• Energy efficiency can be achieved by choosing all the machine movements to be servo-motor
driven & the heaters to be either quartz or flash type fired by special heater controller with fast
response time
• Machines are designed to be easy to maintain and use only standard international make of
components that are available in your region
Quote no.: MT2023241 www.machinecraft.org page 1 of 12

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
a. Description of the Machines
I. Machine-Startup
a. Universal Sheet Size Setting Frames
§ The sheet frames have to be adjusted each time the sheet size changes – this is done using a
universal sheet size setting system directly via the HMI
§ The product changeover time reduces to less than 10 minutes & is complete automatic
Quote no.: MT2023241 www.machinecraft.org page 2 of 12

505 Palm Springs Link Road Malad W Mumbai 400064 INDIA
§ The operator can enter the sheet size on the touchscreen HMI and the bottom and top frames –
driven by 4 x servo motors in bottom and 2 x servo motors in top – adjust automatically
§ There are CNC machined pusher boxes with Linear Rail Guides that move front & back by the
connected servo motor that move the universal frames
§ The universal frames are made using aluminium & brass – CNC machined with special design
§ The cross-members will have spiked teeth to pierce the sheet on 2 x sides – left & right & 32 mm
thick Steel Flat part in front & back
§ The top frame has 8 x pneumatic cylinders that are used to pickup the final formed part and place it
over the automatic sheet loader for part unloading automatically – there is a groove in the bottom
plate in which the lifter mechanism fits in to lift the part
§ Clamping edge size: 25 mm (left & right each side) and 50 mm (front & back each side)
§ The Universal Frames can achieve a reduction of 1000 mm in X direction & 500 mm in Y direction
as standard
b. Quick Tool Clamping - Optional

clamped on the machine and is always in the centre
